Article Title: After DU, JNU asks its students, staff to avoid Jantar Mantar protest site
Article Text:
The university has also asked to exercise responsibility on social media.

NEW DELHI: The Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) Friday asked its "all stakeholers" to "refrain from participating in or visiting gatherings at or around Jantar Mantar" that has been witnessing mobilisation against the perpetual exam paper leaks and the alleged irregularities in the education system.The university has also asked to "exercise responsibility" on social media as violations may "invite legal consequences under applicable laws as well as disciplinary action under the University’s code of conduct"."Advisory All stakeholders of JNU’s epistemic community are advised to act responsibly and prioritize their personal safety. In accordance with the Hon’ble Supreme Court of India’s directions on public demonstrations, you are requested to refrain from participating in or visiting gatherings at or around Jantar Mantar, New Delhi," the University posted on X.It futher wrote: "Please exercise responsibility on social media. Violations may invite legal consequences under applicable laws as well as disciplinary action under the University’s code of conduct. You are also encouraged to uphold the values of academic responsibility and responsible citizenship."Earlier, the University of Delhi (DU) has issued an official safety advisory, urging all students and faculty members to strictly refrain from participating in unauthorised assemblies and demonstrations at Jantar Mantar.The notification comes amid heightened concerns regarding student safety, legal consequences, and the rapid spread of misinformation surrounding recent events in the national capital.In a post on X, the university reminded students that demonstrations at Jantar Mantar are strictly regulated under the directives of the Hon'ble Supreme Court of India. Participating in unlawful assemblies could lead to direct legal action by law enforcement authorities.Highlighting the broader ramifications of legal involvement, the administration warned that police action or legal records could severely compromise students' academic progress, campus standings, and future career opportunities.The administration flagged a surge in fake news, altered media, and misleading online content designed to provoke emotions and escalate tensions. Students have been advised to exercise extreme caution and verify information before reacting or sharing.Taking strong exception to Delhi University's notice, Congress leader Rahul Gandhi lashed out at the univerity for "threatening" the students for exercising the democratic rights."How dare you threaten students for exercising their democratic rights? Please note, you are the ones that will be held accountable when the time comes," Congress leader Rahul Gandh worte on X.
Article Summary: Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) Friday asked its "all stakeholers" to "refrain from participating in or visiting gatherings at or around Jantar Mantar" The university has also asked to exercise responsibility on social media.

Article Title: 'Trademark arrogance': Sonia Gandhi says Modi govt treated NEET protesters as 'enemies of nation'
Article Text:
Sonia Gandhi alleged that even students heading home were not spared as police used batons indiscriminately.

Sonia alleges 'utter impunity'

PM Modi vows stricter law

NEW DELHI: Congress leader Sonia Gandhi on Friday launched a sharp attack on the Narendra Modi government, accusing it of responding to students' demands for accountability and education reforms with "cowardice and wanton cruelty" instead of meaningful action.She described July 20, 2026, as "a day of infamy", alleging that the Delhi Police and Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) used lathis and tear gas to disperse peaceful demonstrators. She also claimed that many protesters were injured and that even students returning home were allegedly beaten.Sonia Gandhi alleged that numerous peaceful protesters were injured, and even students heading home were not spared, as police used batons indiscriminately."The nation has seen some of the most depressing visuals of youth whose bodies have been scarred by pellets, the use of which would undoubtedly have been approved by Union home minister Amit Shah himself," the ex-Congress chief wrote in an article titled 'An education system's collapse, young India's trauma', published in The Hindu.Gandhi said the government's handling of the ongoing student protests reflected its unwillingness to engage with the concerns of young people."The videos from that day are seared into our memories and have stirred our collective conscience. Only the government and its captive media are spinning stories around this spontaneous rising tide of protest. It is time to say loud and clear: Stop, these are our sons and daughters, our young men and women," Gandhi said.She said students across the country had been demanding accountability over examination paper leaks and broader reforms in the education system, but alleged that the Centre had chosen repression over dialogue."The student protests are a natural consequence of these interrelated forces: today's bleak education landscape, which evokes only hopelessness, and the Modi government's trademark arrogance and unwillingness to be accountable or constructive," Gandhi wrote.She further alleged that the government had weakened India's education system while acting with "utter impunity, supreme self-delusion, and abject insensitivity".According to Gandhi, it was the moral, political and constitutional responsibility of every public representative to stand with students and protect their future.Referring to the police action against protesters, she claimed the use of force was consistent with the government's previous response to public demonstrations."We all remember vividly how armed policemen and paramilitary forces ravaged university campuses during the Citizenship (Amendment) Act-National Register of Citizens protests in 2020. The unacceptable excesses against India's women wrestlers can never be erased from our memories," she said.Gandhi also criticised the police action during the recent protests over the alleged NEET paper leak and the state of the education system."Their demands have been straightforward and to the point: accountability from the Government of India and education reform. The Narendra Modi government has responded to their courage with cowardice and wanton cruelty, treating them not as inheritors of the future but as enemies of the nation," she said.Sonia's remarks came a day after Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the Union Cabinet would discuss a Bill to strengthen the existing anti-cheating law with stricter penalties for those involved in examination paper leaks.In a recorded message released late on Thursday night, the Prime Minister said the proposed legislation would include provisions for fast-track courts and tougher punishment for offenders. He said the Bill would be finalised after Cabinet approval and introduced in Parliament when the second week of the Monsoon Session begins.Modi said the government had been working for the past two and a half months to address concerns arising from the alleged NEET paper leak. He added that the Centre had ensured that nearly 22 lakh students did not lose an academic year by conducting the re-examination and declaring the results.The Prime Minister's announcement came shortly after the government replaced the Education Secretary amid the continuing protests over the alleged paper leak.Amid ongoing protests, the government named animal husbandry secretary Naresh Pal Gangwar the new secretary for higher education, while TK Anil Kumar the new school education secretary. Incumbent Vineet Joshi, who had charge of both, moved to low-profile panchayati raj department.Officials said the Prime Minister's Office (PMO) had remained in close touch with the education ministry over issues related to the NEET re-test and the implementation of recommendations made by the K Radhakrishnan Committee on reforms to the National Testing Agency (NTA).
Article Summary: Congress leader Sonia Gandhi launches a sharp attack on the Narendra Modi government. She accuses it of responding to students' demands for accountability and education reforms with "cowardice and wanton cruelty" Gandhi described July 20, 2026, as "a day of infamy", alleging that the Delhi Police and Central Armed Police Forces used lathis and tear gas to disperse peaceful demonstrators.

Article Title: Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike; PM Modi assures more stricter actions on paper leaks- top developments
Article Text:
Sonam Wangchuk ends his hunger strike; PM Modi pushes for Bill for fast track courts against paper leaks

Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike

PM Modi pushes for tougher anti-paper leak law

NEW DELHI: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike early Friday after the Centre assured him of action on key demands related to the student protests, while Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the government would bring a new Bill with stringent provisions against examination paper leaks in a late night video. The proposed legislation, which includes fast-track courts and tougher punishment for offenders, will be discussed by the Union Cabinet on Friday before being introduced in Parliament next week.Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his indefinite hunger strike shortly after midnight at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram in the presence of Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh. He said the decision followed prolonged negotiations with the government and was taken "in view of possible violence in the country.""Just now in the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda & Dr Jitendra Singh and the senior leaders of Apex Body of Leh Ladakh I finally broke my fast after 26 days. Earlier 65 Members of Parliament from different political parties had visited or signed letters urging me to break the fast. This was done after a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country. Meanwhile I urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," Wangchuk posted on X.Following Wangchuk's announcement, Prime Minister Narendra Modi wished him a speedy recovery, saying, "I urge Sonam ji to follow his daily routine as advised by the doctors and regain his weight as soon as possible. I pray to the Almighty that Sonam ji remains healthy."Read more: Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike after 26 days Meanwhile, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke expressed relief that Wangchuk ended his hunger strike but stated that the protest will continue. "We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days. Thank you, sir, for your extraordinary courage and sacrifice. By putting your own life on the line, you awakened the conscience of an entire nation. Your life is far too precious to this country. The Cockroach Janta Party’s peaceful protest at Jantar Mantar will continue until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns," he said on X.In a late-night video message, Prime Minister Modi announced that the government would introduce a Bill with stringent provisions against paper leaks, including fast-track courts and harsher punishment for offenders. He said the proposal would be discussed by the Union Cabinet on Friday and the government would try to get it passed in Parliament next week."The Cabinet will discuss the issue of paper leaks on Friday... On Monday, when the second week of the Monsoon Session of Parliament begins, a Bill will be brought with provisions for strong action against paper leaks and we will try to pass it as early as possible," PM Modi said.Calling paper leaks "very painful" for lakhs of students and their families, the Prime Minister said the government had acted swiftly after the alleged NEET-UG paper leak."The guilty have been caught. They are now in jail. Our most significant responsibility was to ensure that the students do not lose a year... By using all resources, the government has ensured that 22 lakh students could attend the re-test," he said.PM Modi added that he had directed authorities to establish fast-track courts to ensure swift justice and said the proposed legislation would be another step towards making the examination system "foolproof" and protecting students' interests.Read more: 'More strict actions to come against paper leaks' - PM Modi announces new Bill push
Article Summary: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his indefinite hunger strike shortly after midnight at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram. Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the government would bring a new Bill with stringent provisions against examination paper leaks. The proposed legislation, which includes fast-track courts and tougher punishment for offenders, will be discussed by the Union Cabinet on Friday.

Article Title: Honeymoon murder case: SC cancels Sonam Raghuvanshi's bail, orders her to surrender in three weeks
Article Text:
The order came after the Meghalaya government challenged the Meghalaya high court's decision to grant Sonam bail.

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you agree with the decision to set aside Sonam Raghuvanshi's bail? Yes, it's fair No, she should remain free 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Thursday set aside the bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi, the prime accused in the alleged murder of her husband, Raja Raghuvanshi, during their honeymoon in Meghalaya in May 2025. The apex court directed her to surrender before the trial court within three weeks, PTI reported.“We are inclined to observe that continued enlargement on bail of the respondent (Sonam Raghuvanshi) at this stage might hinder the ongoing trial. In such a matter we are inclined to set-aside the bail order... The respondent has been given two weeks to surrender. In the event of trial not concluding within six months the respondent can submit an application seeking bail,” the Court observed, as quoted by news agency ANI.The order came after the Meghalaya government challenged the Meghalaya high court's decision to grant Sonam bail. The high court had upheld the trial court's order, holding that she was entitled to bail because the mandatory "grounds of arrest" had not been furnished to her at the time of arrest.During an earlier hearing, a bench of Justices MM Sundresh and PB Varale had offered Sonam two options: Either surrender to facilitate the examination of public witnesses while the appeal against her bail was pending, or contest the Meghalaya government's appeal on merits."You (Sonam's counsel) have two options. Either we will pass an order on merits or we will ask you to surrender, let the public witnesses question you - meanwhile we will decide the matter on merits (on bail)," the bench had observed. The judges had also advised her counsel that "surrender" would be the better course.Opposing the bail, Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, appearing for the Meghalaya government, argued that Sonam had voluntarily surrendered before the police and therefore could not later claim that the grounds of arrest had not been provided. He also maintained that the error in the arrest memo was merely typographical, with Section 403(1) being mentioned instead of Section 103(1) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, which deals with murder.
Article Summary: The Supreme Court on Thursday set aside the bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi. The apex court directed her to surrender before the trial court within three weeks. The order came after the Meghalaya government challenged the high court's decision to grant Sonam bail.

Article Title: CJP protest: Delhi Metro shuts 17 stations for third day; see full list
Article Text:
17 Delhi Metro stations will be closed for entry and exit from Friday morning.

The stations that will remain closed are:

Lok Kalyan Marg

Rajiv Chowk

Patel Chowk

Ramakrishna Ashram Marg

Barakhambha Road

Supreme Court

Seva Teerth

Janpath

Mandi House

Central Secretariat

ITO

Delhi Gate

Indraprastha

Khan Market

Jor Bagh

Shivaji Stadium

Jhandewalan





NEW DELHI: The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) on Friday announced that 17 metro stations across the national capital will remain closed for entry and exit from 7.30am on Friday until further instructions, citing "security reasons".The latest advisory marks the third consecutive day of metro disruptions in central Delhi amid the ongoing Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led protest at Jantar Mantar.In a post on X, the DMRC said interchange facilities will remain available at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House and Central Secretariat despite the closures.The decision comes a day after the DMRC shut 17 stations on Thursday, following the closure of 16 stations on Wednesday, when hundreds of office-goers, students and other commuters were stranded during the morning rush hour.Entry gates at Mandi House, Rajiv Chowk and Central Secretariat were reopened later on Thursday night.The repeated closures come amid heightened security across central Delhi following the July 20 'Sansad Chalo' march, which witnessed clashes between protesters and police near Parliament.Since then, authorities have maintained heavy security deployment around Jantar Mantar, Parliament and adjoining areas, with prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) remaining in force across the New Delhi district.The CJP has continued its sit-in at Jantar Mantar, demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET paper leak and broader reforms in the examination system.The DMRC has advised commuters to plan their journeys accordingly, adding that normal operations at the affected stations will resume only after further instructions.
Article Summary: 17 Delhi Metro stations will remain closed for entry and exit from 7.30am on Friday until further instructions, citing "security reasons" The latest advisory marks the third consecutive day of metro disruptions in central Delhi amid the ongoing Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led protest at Jantar Mantar.

Article Title: Won’t forget Indian military’s rescue of 12 Chinese crew members after ship fire: China's defence attache
Article Text:
Defence Attache to India, Rear Admiral You Jian thanked Indian military for rescuing 12 Chinese crew members last year

NEW DELHI: China’s newly appointed Defence Attache to India, Rear Admiral You Jian, has thanked the Indian military for rescuing 12 Chinese crew members from a cargo ship that caught fire last year, saying Beijing “will not forget” the operation."We will not forget that, last June, the Indian Military rescued 12 Chinese crew members from a cargo ship that caught fire and exploded off the coast of Kerala," You said while speaking at a reception marking the 99th anniversary of the founding of the Chinese People’s Liberation Army (PLA). The Indian Navy and the Indian Coast Guard had jointly carried out the rescue operation to save the lives of the Chinese crew.A senior Chinese military officer lauding the effort of the Indian military is aimed at improving the relations between the two countries. New Delhi and Beijing have engaged in multiple constructive and forward-looking discussions towards gradual normalisation after the 2020 LAC faceoff.The Chinese defence attache also called for closer communication and cooperation between the armed forces of the two countries. He also highlighted the role played by the armed forces of both countries in United Nations peacekeeping operations. “We need to remember that both the Chinese and Indian armed forces have made outstanding contributions and great sacrifices in UN peacekeeping operations.We have always believed that the common interests between our two countries far outweigh our differences,” You said.On deepening military engagement, he said, “We are willing to work with the Indian side to maintain communication through diplomatic and military channels, enhance mutual trust and promote cooperation through dialogue, and contribute to the well-being of the peoples of China and India.”
Article Summary: China’s newly appointed Defence Attache to India, Rear Admiral You Jian, has thanked the Indian military for rescuing 12 Chinese crew members from a cargo ship that caught fire last year. The Indian Navy and the Indian Coast Guard had jointly carried out the rescue operation to save the lives of the Chinese crew.

Article Title: 400 Pakistani handles revived from Op Sindoor target NEET protests, being blocked: Delhi Police
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Delhi Police on Thursday said it had identified and blocked more than 400 Pakistani social media handles allegedly spreading misinformation and rumours amid the ongoing protests, urging people not to fall for fake content circulating online.Addressing the media, deputy commissioner of Police (DCP) Sumit Jha said the same social media accounts had also been active during Operation Sindoor and were now attempting to exploit the current situation to provoke people and distract students."We have noticed that some attempts are being made to spread misinformation on social media. So far, more than 400 Pakistani social media handles have been identified, which are taking advantage of this situation and spreading fake content and rumors. They are being blocked. These are the same social media handles which were active during Operation Sindoor as well. Their only aim is to provoke people and especially divert the attention of students. "Jha appealed to the public, particularly students and parents, not to be influenced by unverified posts, edited videos or content shared by anonymous accounts."I appeal to you that for the safety of children, do not get provoked by any fake post, edited videos or any post made by anonymous handle. Fact check any post before sharing it. Do not spread rumors and do not let any anti-social elements control your emotions."He also reiterated that the police respected citizens' right to peaceful protest."Remember that it is your right to hold a peaceful protest and Delhi Police respects this right."
Article Summary: Delhi Police says it has identified and blocked more than 400 Pakistani social media handles allegedly spreading misinformation and rumours. Deputy commissioner of Police Sumit Jha said the same social media accounts had also been active during Operation Sindoor and were now attempting to exploit the current situation to provoke people and distract students.

Article Title: 'In view of possible violence in the country': Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike after 26 days; PM Modi 'prays for his health'
Article Text:
Sonam Wangchuk ended his hunger strike after 26 days

NEW DELHI: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk on Friday ended his 26-day hunger strike in the presence of Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, saying the decision followed prolonged negotiations and was taken to avoid any possibility of violence in the country.In a post on X, Wangchuk said he broke his fast in the presence of the two Union ministers and senior leaders of the Apex Body of Leh Ladakh. He said 65 MPs from different political parties had either visited him or signed letters urging him to end the fast."This was done after a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country. I will explain the conditions in detail in a separate video very soon. Meanwhile, I urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," Wangchuk said. PM Modi wished the activist good health. "I urge Sonam ji to follow his routine as per the doctors' advice and regain his old weight as soon as possible. I pray to the Lord that Sonam ji remains healthy," the Prime Minister said.Meanwhile, Cockroach Janata Party, with whom Wangchuk had aligned with when he started the hunger strike, said, "We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days.Thank you, sir, for your extraordinary courage and sacrifice. By putting your own life on the line, you awakened the conscience of an entire nation. Your life is far too precious to this country," said founder Abhijeet Dipke.Wangchuk had been shifted from Delhi's Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram following directions from the Delhi High Court.Meanwhile, PM Modi also released a video message addressing students protesting over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. Calling the issue "very painful" for lakhs of students and their families, the Prime Minister said the Union Cabinet would consider stricter measures against paper leaks, including provisions for fast-track courts and harsher punishment for offenders, with the government aiming to introduce the legislation in Parliament during the ongoing Monsoon Session. He also said the government's immediate priority after the alleged leak was to ensure that nearly 22 lakh students did not lose an academic year by conducting the re-examination at the earliest.On the other hand, the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) has called to continue nationwide "peaceful protests" demanding the resignation of union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. "The Cockroach Janta Party’s peaceful protest at Jantar Mantar will continue until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns," said Dipke in the statement about Wangchuk ending his hunger strike.The Centre had reiterated its willingness to hold talks with student representatives. Union minister Jitendra Singh said the government had sent four formal proposals for discussions and that he and Union Health Minister JP Nadda would be present during the talks.However, CJP chief spokesperson Saurav Das said the party had not received any communication from the government and remained ready for talks at Jantar Mantar or a neutral venue such as the Constitution Club of India.Read more: 'More strict actions to come against paper leaks' - PM Modi announces new Bill push
Article Summary: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk on Friday ended his 26-day hunger strike. He said the decision followed prolonged negotiations and was taken to avoid any possibility of violence in the country. PM Modi wished the activist good health. Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) has called to continue nationwide "peaceful protests"

Article Title: 2,500 detained, homes of 2 LeT terrorists razed in Kashmir after cop killing
Article Text:
2 LeT terrorists arrested

SRINAGAR: Security forces demolished family homes of two “wanted” Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorists and detained more than 2,500 people for questioning across Kashmir overnight in a sweeping crackdown following Wednesday’s fatal shooting of a J&K police head constable on duty in Lal Chowk market in Anantnag town.Officials said Thursday forces used controlled explosives to demolish the house where Adil Ahmad Thoker’s family lives in Guri village of Anantnag district. It was the second time the house was blown up after a similar demolition following the Pahalgam terrorist attack that killed 26 people on April 22, 2025.Forces also demolished the house of Haroon Rashid Ganaie’s family at Hassanpora Tawella in Bijbehara. Teams reached both villages around 3am and asked occupants to leave before detonating explosives. Thoker and Ganaie have been associated with LeT since 2018.Police said those detained were suspected “overground workers” accused of providing logistics, shelter, cash, transport, communications and other support to terrorists. About 700 were detained in Srinagar district, 178 in Baramulla, 200 in Budgam, 100 in Ganderbal, and several hundred more in Anantnag, Shopian and Kulgam.A police officer said the operation aimed to identify and dismantle networks providing logistical, financial, communication, transport and shelter support to terrorist organisations.LG Manoj Sinha chaired a security review Thursday and directed officials to remain “highly vigilant” and neutralise emerging threats. He also reviewed arrangements for Amarnath Yatra, suspended since July 19 because of heavy rain. More than 391,000 have visited the cave shrine in Anantnag district in the first 20 days of the annual pilgrimage.Officials said the manhunt continues for the lone terrorist who murdered 45-year-old head constable Ashiq Hussain Qureshi, a father of three from Lalpora village in central Kashmir’s Budgam.CM Omar Abdullah criticised the demolitions and detentions, calling the police action counter-productive. “People are angry that a police head constable has been killed so brutally. But when the number of arrests rises from hundreds to 2,000 or 2,500, that public anger risks being redirected against us,” he said. “So, demolishing houses or making thousands of arrests will not help improve the situation.”Peoples Conference chairman Sajad Lone and PDP president Mehbooba Mufti called the detentions “mass arrests”, saying they “look more like community punishment”.
Article Summary: Security forces demolish homes of two 'wanted' Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorists. More than 2,500 people detained for questioning across Kashmir overnight. Crackdown follows fatal shooting of a J&K police head constable on duty.

Article Title: 'More strict actions to come against paper leaks': PM Modi announces new Bill push in video message
Article Text:
Prime Minister assures students that strict action will be taken against paper leaks on Friday

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday announced that government will bring in more strict actions against paper leaks. In a late night video message, PM Modi said that the Union Cabinet will discuss these stringent measures on Friday and the government will try to get the Bill passed in Parliament next week.He assured that the Centre will bring in stricter measures to curb examination paper leaks, including provisions for fast-track courts and harsher punishments for offenders.His message comes amid massive nationwide protests against alleged irregularities in the NEET UG examination. "I know that paper leak is not an ordinary issue. It is very painful for lakh of students and their guardians.""Numerous effective measures have been taken in the past two and a half months since the paper leak incident. The culprits have been caught and are in jail. Our most important responsibility was to ensure that students' year is not wasted. The government used all its might to ensure that 22 lakh students could take their exams in the shortest possible time," he added.Further talking about the bill on fast track courts he added, "On the 19th July, the results were declared. I directed the departments today to establish a fast-track court. The departments presented me with the resolution late at night. This resolution, along with the provision for a fast-track court and harsh punishment, will be discussed in the cabinet tomorrow. After the suggestions of cabinet colleagues, it will be finalised, and since the second week of Parliament is starting from Monday, efforts will be made to get that bill passed in the House as soon as possible. "This after PM's remarks, at the NDA parliamentary party meeting on Tuesday, saying that the govt is working to make the exam system “foolproof”.
Article Summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi assures students that strict action will be taken against paper leaks on Friday. Union Cabinet will discuss these stringent measures on Friday and the government will try to get the Bill passed in Parliament next week. His message comes amid massive nationwide protests against alleged irregularities in the NEET UG examination.

Article Title: SC laments vanishing of joint family system
Article Text:
SC expressed concern about the vanishing of the joint family system

Expressing concern about the vanishing of the joint family system and parents in nuclear families not spending quality time with their kids and instead handing over gadgets to keep them busy, Supreme Court Thursday said that it would lead to "disaster" as the new generation would be unaware of family bonding and relationships.While analysing the reasons behind recent incidents of people killing spouses, including the case of Sonam Raghuvanshi, a bench of Justices M M Sundresh and P B Varale said that it was a reflection of changing society. It said that the present generation is more intelligent and informative, but it does not know how to deal with pressure. The court said that it is unfortunate that people nowadays rely on WhatsApp-circulated messages and treat them as knowledge. Agreeing with the court's concern, solicitor general Tushar Mehta said that it has become a social problem and trust in human relationships is diminishing and there is a lack of tolerance.Justice Sundresh said that he was recently in a restaurant and he saw that a family gave a phone to a kid in order to keep him busy while they were having food. "If parents are replaced by gadgets, then it is a disaster. It is going to happen more in future. Joint family is an asset, and people don't understand it. Happiness does not come with material possession but by human bonding and relationship. We are becoming like machines," he said.
Article Summary: Justices M M Sundresh and P B Varale said that the present generation is more intelligent and informative, but it does not know how to deal with pressure. The court said that it is unfortunate that people nowadays rely on WhatsApp- circulated messages and treat them as knowledge.

Article Title: India invites Bangladesh PM to Brics Summit
Article Text:
India invites Bangladesh PM

DHAKA/NEW DELHI: As part of Delhi's broader approach to enhancing global collaboration and cooperation, India has invited PM Tarique Rahman to attend the 18th Brics Summit, which will focus on global cooperation, trade, and sustainable development. India will host the summit in New Delhi on September 12-13.Bangladesh state minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam said Thursday that the ministry had received the invitation from the Indian side and forwarded it to the Prime Minister's Office, without elaborating further.PM Narendra Modi had invited Tarique in Feb too, after he took office. Tarique has so far not appeared inclined to take the political risk of visiting India while former PM Sheikh Hasina remains on Indian soil, amid concerns about a domestic backlash. However, Bangladesh also aspires to become a Brics member and has already received support from China and Russia.Both Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin are expected to attend the summit.Tarique recently visited Malaysia and China, and also plans to travel to Japan and the US for the UN General Assembly later this year. An official confirmation of India's invitation is still awaited.India had also invited Bangladesh in 2016, the last time it hosted the Brics summit in person, as a Bimstec member for a Brics outreach session.External affairs minister S Jaishankar on Thursday met Bangladesh foreign minister Khalilur Rahman on the sidelines of the Asean Regional Forum Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Manila. An official in Dhaka said the two ministers exchanged greetings.Responding to a question on regional instability, Shama Obaed said Bangladesh, as a democratic state with a democratically elected govt, wanted peace and stability in the region. She added that Bangladesh expected India, as a large democratic country, to address its internal issues and move towards a solution.India's Brics chairship is guided by the theme "Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability", reflecting a people-centric approach articulated by Modi at the 2025 Rio Summit. Brics comprises Brazil, China, Egypt, Ethiopia, India, Indonesia, Iran, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Africa and the UAE as members.The 17th Brics Summit was held on July 6-7, 2025, in Rio de Janeiro in Brazil.
Article Summary: India has invited Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman to attend the 18th Brics Summit. India will host the summit in New Delhi on September 12-13. Bangladesh also aspires to become a Brics member and has already received support from China and Russia.

Article Title: 'No cases against peaceful protesters': List of govt assurances that ended Sonam Wangchuk's fast
Article Text:
Wangchuk announced the end of his fast after Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram around midnight and conveyed the government's assurances.

NEW DELHI: Activist Sonam Wangchuk on Friday ended his 26-day indefinite hunger strike after receiving assurances from the Centre that key demands raised by students protesting over alleged examination irregularities would be addressed.In a post on X, Wangchuk said the government had assured that there would be a discussion in Parliament on accountability in the examination system, compensation for families of students who died by suicide following the recent NEET paper leak, and no cases against peaceful protesters."END OF HUNGER... BEGINNING OF ACCOUNTABILITY...!" Wangchuk wrote.He added, "Sonam Wangchuk ends his fast after 26 days upon getting assurance from the government and members of parliament of all political parties that the issue of accountability in the failing examination system will be discussed on the floors of the Indian Parliament.""The government also gave assurance that families of suicide victims in the recent NEET examination paper leak will be given adequate compensation and that there will be no cases registered against the peacefully protesting students and youths," he said.Wangchuk announced the end of his fast after Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram around midnight and conveyed the government's assurances."Just now in the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda, Dr Jitendra Singh and the senior leaders of Apex Body of Leh Ladakh, I finally broke my fast after 26 days," Wangchuk wrote.He said 65 Members of Parliament from different political parties had either visited him or signed letters urging him to end the fast."This was done after long negotiations on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country. I will explain the conditions in detail in a separate video very soon. Meanwhile, I urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," he said.Prime Minister Narendra Modi welcomed Wangchuk's decision to end the fast. In a post on X, he said, "I urge Sonam ji to follow his daily routine as advised by the doctors and regain his weight as soon as possible.""I pray to the Almighty that Sonam ji remains healthy," the Prime Minister added.The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), which has been leading the protest at Jantar Mantar, welcomed Wangchuk's decision but said its agitation would continue until Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.Wangchuk joined the CJP-led protest on June 28 and began an indefinite hunger strike in support of demands for accountability over alleged irregularities in competitive examinations, reforms to the education system and Pradhan's resignation. During the fast, he lost around 11 kg and continued the protest from Medanta Hospital after being shifted there from Safdarjung Hospital following legal proceedings.
Article Summary: Wangchuk announced the end of his fast after Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram around midnight. He said 65 Members of Parliament from different political parties had either visited him or signed letters urging him to end the fast.

Article Title: 'Don't insult students' intelligence': Rahul Gandhi slams PM Modi's midnight video on paper leaks
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Leader of opposition in Lok Sabha, Rahul Gandhi early Friday attacked Prime Minister Narendra Modi accusing him of sidestepping the demand for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan 's resignation over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak.Reacting to the Prime Minister's late-night video message on the issue, Rahul said PM Modi should not "insult the intelligence" of students and demanded Pradhan be sacked saying the PM's address failed to raise students' key concerns.In a post on X, Rahul wrote, "Mr Modi, our students are demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Don't insult their intelligence with this pathetic midnight video."Listing his demands, he said:"1. Sack Pradhan.2. Punish those who beat students.3. Apologise."The leader of oppostion's remarks came hours after Prime Minister Modi announced that the government would bring a Bill in Parliament next week providing for stringent punishment against those involved in examination paper leaks.In a self-recorded video shared on X late Thursday night, the Prime Minister said the proposed legislation, which includes provisions for Fast-Track Courts and stricter penalties, would be discussed by the Union Cabinet on Friday before being introduced in Parliament when the second week of the Monsoon Session begins on Monday."Friends, I know that paper leaks are not an ordinary matter. For lakhs of students and their parents, it is extremely painful," PM Modi said."Our most important responsibility was to ensure that a whole year for the students is not wasted. It was very important to conduct the exams immediately. The government used its full strength to arrange exams for nearly 22 lakh students in the shortest possible time. And just five or six days ago, on the 19th, the results were also announced," he said.The Prime Minister said the government was now moving towards stronger legal action."Today I have issued directions to the departments to set up Fast-Track Courts. The departments worked tirelessly and, late last night, provided me with a draft. This draft, which includes provisions for Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishments, will be discussed in the Cabinet tomorrow," he said.He added that after incorporating suggestions from Cabinet colleagues, the Bill would be finalised and introduced in Parliament next week."Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth. Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared," PM Modi said.The opposition has been demanding Pradhan's resignation and a detailed discussion on the alleged NEET-UG paper leak during the Monsoon Session, with the issue triggering protests both inside Parliament and on the streets.Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike on Friday at Medanta Hospital in the presence of Union ministers J P Nadda and Jitendra Singh, along with senior leaders of the Apex Body of Leh.In a post on X, Wangchuk said he decided to end the fast after prolonged discussions and in view of the possibility of violence."Just now in the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda & Dr. Jitendra Singh and the senior leaders of Apex Body of Leh Ladakh I finally broke my fast after 26 days... This was done after a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country," he said."I will explain the conditions in detail in a separate video very soon. Meanwhile urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," he added.Meanwhile, the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) has called for nationwide peaceful protests on Friday, reiterating its demand for Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the alleged examination irregularities.
Article Summary: Rahul Gandhi accuses Prime Minister Narendra Modi of sidestepping the demand for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the alleged NEET- UG 2026 paper leak. The leader of oppostion's remarks came hours after PM Modi announced that the government would bring a Bill in Parliament next week providing for stringent punishment.

Article Title: Opposition MPs take out march in solidarity with protesters
Article Text:
Opposition MPs stage protest against govt at Parliament in black clothes

NEW DELHI: Opposition MPs took out a 'candlelight march' Thursday to the Gandhi Smriti in solidarity with the protesting students, two days after they held a dharna outside the PM's residence and were forcibly evacuated by police.The MPs belonging to Congress, SP, TMC, Left parties, RJD, Shiv Sena and others met at Rahul Gandhi's residence. From there, they marched to the Gandhi Smriti holding their phone torches. The marchers included Rahul, K C Venugopal, Ramjilal Suman, Supriya Sule, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, N K Premachandran, John Brittas, Sandosh Kumar, Sudhakar Singh, Misa Bharti, Arvind Sawant, Saugata Roy and Mahua Moitra.Rahul said, "We go to remember the students we lost - the children driven to take their own lives after the NEET paper leak. And we go to stand with the students who lie injured today - beaten for peacefully demanding justice and accountability. India's students are not alone. The entire opposition stands with them and their demands."Congress's P Chidambaram said, "All roads are barricaded, and hundreds of policemen are mobilised to 'secure' the roads of Delhi. What is govt afraid of? Why this sense of panic?" Earlier, Congress rubbished PM Modi's statement assuring the protesting youth. Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge said the students don't need platitudes after govt used pellet guns, shock batons and tear gas on them.
Article Summary: Opposition MPs took out a 'candlelight march' Thursday to the Gandhi Smriti in solidarity with the protesting students. The MPs belonging to Congress, SP, TMC, Left parties, RJD, Shiv Sena and others met at Rahul Gandhi's residence. The marchers included Rahul, K C Venugopal, Ramjilal Suman and Supriya Sule.

Article Title: CJP's Dipke: Sonam Wangchuk's Hunger Strike Ends, Protest Continues Until Pradhan Resigns
Article Text:
"We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days. Thank you, sir, for your extraordinary courage and sacrifice. By putting your own life on the line, you awakened the conscience of an entire nation. Your life is far too precious to this country,"

"The Cockroach Janta Party's peaceful protest at Jantar Mantar will continue until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns,"

Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike

NEW DELHI: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke on Friday expressed relief after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike, while making it clear that its peaceful agitation at Delhi's Jantar Mantar would continue until Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.In a post on X, Dipke thanked Wangchuk for what he described as an "extraordinary courage and sacrifice" during the fast.Dipke wrote.However, he further said the end of Wangchuk's fast would not mark the end of the movement.Dipke said.Wangchuk had been on an indefinite hunger strike over alleged irregularities in the NEET examination and the government's handling of the issue.Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his indefinite hunger strike shortly after midnight at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram in the presence of Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh.He said the decision followed prolonged negotiations with the government and was taken "in view of possible violence in the country.""Just now in the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda & Dr Jitendra Singh and the senior leaders of Apex Body of Leh Ladakh I finally broke my fast after 26 days. Earlier 65 Members of Parliament from different political parties had visited or signed letters urging me to break the fast. This was done after a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country. Meanwhile I urge you all to stay very vigilant about not allowing violence of any kind anywhere," Wangchuk posted on X.The CJP protest intensified on July 20 when the 'Sansad Chalo' march towards Parliament ended in clashes between protesters and Delhi Police after demonstrators allegedly attempted to breach barricades. The agitation has since continued at Jantar Mantar demanding the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan.The latest security measures, including the temporary suspension of mobile internet services and the early closure of commercial establishments in Connaught Place, come as authorities remain on alert amid concerns over further escalation around the protest site and adjoining parts of Central Delhi.
Article Summary: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke expressed relief after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike. Dipke thanked Wangchuk for what he described as an "extraordinary courage and sacrifice" during the fast. Wangchuk had been on an indefinite hunger strike over alleged irregularities in the NEET examination.

Article Title: Low-key Nadda emerges as govt point man in talks with protesters
Article Text:
Nadda emerges as key negotiator from govt's side

NEW DELHI: As the govt's main interlocutor with students protesting under the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) banner, Union health minister JP Nadda has been thrust into the limelight, holding talks with them and visiting the injured in hospitals.This is an interesting turn in Nadda's journey. The former BJP president is not really known for revelling in the spotlight and is more at ease in closed-door political meetings than being in the middle of public attention. That his comments rarely stoke any controversy or ruffle others is being considered an asset when govt is holding talks with a group which is riding the angst of the student community, as any misstep may inflame passions.Nadda cut his teeth in students' politics as an ABVP activist in Patna and Himachal University and grew in the role to head Bharatiya Janata Yuva Morcha, the youth wing of BJP, collecting long experience of agitational politics, the second nature of student activism, which makes him the right person for the job.BJP circles are of the view that while being health minister who looked after the treatment of injured protesters put Nadda in a position to connect with CJP, his emergence as a negotiator came as a surprise.It is surmised that the former BJP chief's low-key presence added to his acceptability. A BJP functionary said one of his strengths was that he was soft-spoken but also knew where to draw the line, a trait that ensures that he could engage with protestors skilfully while ensuring that the govt's views were also put across firmly.He had also met activist Sonam Wangchuk in a private hospital as part of govt's efforts to persuade him to break his hunger strike and engage in talks to end the stir.Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, known for his political savvy and proven track record of successful political outreach, could have been the other choice but for the fact that his resignation is CJP's main demand.With some other leading BJP figures not really a good fit for the job because of their seniority, the task fell on Nadda, now a member of the party's brains trust.
Article Summary: Nadda cut his teeth in students' politics as an ABVP activist in Patna and Himachal University. He grew in the role to head Bharatiya Janata Yuva Morcha, the youth wing of BJP. It is surmised that the former BJP chief's low-key presence added to his acceptability.

Article Title: Police, SDRF rescue 2 trapped in Tawi river; Army launches relief ops in Reasi, Rajouri
Article Text:
An NDRF personnel evacuates a youth from the middle of the Tawi river

JAMMU: A joint team of J&K Police and SDRF rescued two individuals stranded in the Tawi near Prambli, in Jammu’s Phallian mandal, following a sudden rise in the river’s water level Thursday, even as the Army launched relief and evacuation operations in rain-hit Reasi and Rajouri districts.The duo — Farman Ali (25) from Allora and Farooq Ahmed (28) of Sohanjana — had gone to graze cattle near the river when its water level swelled, leaving them trapped in the middle of the river.After torrential rain triggered landslides and led to the rise in the water level of Arnas river in Reasi, Army troops assisted civilians residing in low-lying areas to move to safer locations, established temporary shelters and provided hot meals, essential supplies, and necessary medical relief in affected areas, defence spokesperson Lt Col Suneel Bartwal said Thursday, adding that a quick reaction team was on standby to respond to any contingency.On receiving information about a civilian suffering from severe dehydration in Panjnara-Peeri area of Rajouri, Army personnel reached the location and conducted a medical assessment before administering first aid, including intravenous fluids, to stabilise the patient’s condition and prevent further deterioration. Following initial treatment, the Army team facilitated the patient’s evacuation for further medical examination and specialised care, Lt Col Bartwal said.A multi-agency operation to trace seven missing people in Murrah, Chang Nallah in Poonch’s Surankote entered the fifth day as round-the-clock searches continued Thursday too. A separate operation to trace a missing employee of the Jal Shakti department also remained underway.Jammu-Srinagar National Highway (NH-44) remained closed for the second consecutive day Thursday. The 250-km NH, the only all-weather path linking J&K with the rest of India, was blocked at multiple places due to rain-triggered landslides, shooting stones, and road erosion between Jakhani in Udhampur district and Banihal in Ramban district.More than 600 vehicles remained stranded in different stretches of the NH, including Ramban, Nagrota, Jammu, Udhampur, Lakhanpur and Samba, due to the closure. Officials said restoration work was underway, but continuous rainfall was hampering repair and clearance efforts. J&K traffic police issued an advisory urging commuters to avoid travelling on the highway until the road is declared clear and safe for traffic.Additionally, over a dozen inter-district roads, including the Doda-Kishtwar road, remained closed due to landslides, mudslides, and erosion. All major pilgrimages — including Amarnath Yatra, Vaishno Devi Yatra, Sarthal Mata Yatra and Machail Mata Yatra — have remained suspended in J&K since July 19. So far, the twin frontier districts of Rajouri-Poonch have lost property worth hundreds of crores to rain-linked destruction. More than 100 vehicles have been washed away, although Army teams have managed to recover a few.
Article Summary: J&K Police and SDRF rescued two individuals stranded in the Tawi near Prambli, in Jammu’s Phallian mandal. The duo — Farman Ali (25) from Allora and Farooq Ahmed (28) of Sohanjana — had gone to graze cattle near the river.

Article Title: CJP delegation to meet government ministers on Friday as Centre renews outreach: Report
Article Text: NEW DELHI: A delegation of the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) will meet government ministers at 12.30 pm on Friday, PTI reported, citing sources, marking the first formal engagement after days of deadlock between the Centre and the student-led outfit over the alleged NEET paper leak.The meeting comes after repeated efforts by the government to initiate dialogue with the protesters. On Thursday, Union minister J P Nadda invited the CJP leadership for talks, but the offer was declined as the outfit insisted that any discussion should take place at a neutral venue near the protest site, according to PTI sources.Government sources had earlier told ANI that an offer to continue talks with the CJP leadership had also been rejected, prolonging the stalemate.CJP spokesperson Saurav Das had maintained that the group was willing to hold talks but not at a minister's residence or office. "We refused to go to anyone's house or office. The people's court is here. Ministers should come among the people. If they want to talk, it should be held at the Jantar Mantar," he had said.Das, however, said the CJP remained open to dialogue at a neutral venue if there were security concerns.The protests, centred at Jantar Mantar, have continued for nearly a month, with demonstrators demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, compensation for families of students who allegedly died by suicide over the NEET paper leak, and withdrawal of cases filed against peaceful protesters.Earlier this week, representatives of the CJP met J P Nadda and submitted their demands. According to the CJP, the Union minister assured them that the issues would be discussed at the appropriate level, ANI reported.
Article Summary: A delegation of the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) will meet government ministers on Friday. It is the first formal engagement after days of deadlock between the Centre and the student-led outfit over the alleged NEET paper leak. The meeting comes after repeated efforts by the government to initiate dialogue with the protesters.

Article Title: ‘Antisocial elements taking advantage’: Sonam Wangchuk urges protesters to remain peaceful, says 'only peace is my way'
Article Text:
File photo: Activist Sonam Wangchuk

Continues fast from hospital

CJP calls for nationwide protests

NEW DELHI: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk on Thursday appealed to supporters to maintain peace during the ongoing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak, saying some "antisocial elements" were trying to provoke violence and urging demonstrators to respond only with non-violence.In a post on X marking the 26th day of his hunger strike, Wangchuk said he was distressed to learn that attempts were being made to derail the peaceful nature of the protests outside Delhi's Jantar Mantar."DAY 26. PEACE & ONLY PEACE IS MY WAY… While at Jantar Mantar the protests remain peaceful I’m pained to learn that elsewhere some antisocial elements are taking advantage of the peaceful protests to provoke violence," Wangchuk wrote."No matter what the other side does our response must only be flowers. Please keep this tradition at all costs," he added.Wangchuk's latest appeal came a day after he was shifted from Delhi's Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram, where he continues his indefinite hunger strike.According to a statement issued by Medanta Hospital, Wangchuk remains "stable and alert", with his vital signs within accepted parameters.The hospital said he is being treated by a multidisciplinary team of doctors and that all medical care is being provided with his informed consent.Earlier on Wednesday, Wangchuk had said he had lost 11 kilograms during his 25-day fast but remained determined to continue unless the government assured that no legal action, including FIRs, would be taken against protesting students."I request the government not to use force against children. I demand an assurance for no accusations or FIRs through which they can be threatened with police or jails," he had said.The activist had also praised students who participated in the Cockroach Janata Party's 'Chalo Sansad' march on July 20, saying they remained peaceful despite police action."Even upon being instigated and some elements being brought in for stone pelting, you remained patient. My heart melted. Because they were attacked brutally, I decided to continue fasting," Wangchuk had said.He added that several ministers and opposition leaders, along with a letter signed by 65 MPs, had urged him to end his hunger strike, but reiterated that he would do so only after receiving assurances that protesters would not face legal action.Meanwhile, the Cockroach Janata Party has called for coordinated peaceful protests across the country on Friday, urging student unions and other organisations to join demonstrations demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the alleged paper leak.The protest call comes as the Centre continues to seek a discussion with the Opposition on the issue without preconditions, while CJP leaders have maintained that the education minister's resignation remains "non-negotiable".
Article Summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk appeals to supporters to maintain peace during the ongoing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak. Wangchuk urges demonstrators to respond only with non-violence. Cockroach Janata Party has called for coordinated peaceful protests across the country on Friday.

Article Title: Watch: Gujarat rains trigger waist-deep flooding, vehicles go under
Article Text:
Gujarat rains wreck havoc

Floodwaters submerge vehicles, roads in Vapi

Relief operations underway, CM reviews situation

Red alert continues, rescue teams on standby

NEW DELHI: Around 1,200 people have been rescued and hundreds more relocated as heavy rains battered south Gujarat, with Umbergaon taluka in Valsad district receiving over 1,100 mm of rainfall in just 16 hours, officials said on Thursday.One rain-related death was reported in Tapi district. The Indian Air Force and Coast Guard have been put on alert for rescue efforts, while NDRF and SDRF teams are deployed across the affected areas.Valsad district was the worst hit, with many talukas receiving over 600 mm of rain during the same period. A red alert for heavy to extremely heavy rainfall was in effect for Valsad, Navsari, Surat and Tapi districts.Large parts of Vapi in Valsad district were inundated, with floodwaters submerging parked vehicles and disrupting normal life. Videos from the area showed waist-deep water in residential colonies and commercial areas, with people wading through the deluge.The Kaveri river in Navsari rose sharply, stranding 200 people across 11 villages. They were rescued and relocated to safety. In Surat, 1,500 residents were moved from low-lying areas and waterlogged localities.Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel visited the State Emergency Operation Centre and reviewed the situation, speaking to district collectors and instructing them to move people from vulnerable areas to safer places.He also spoke to Union Home Minister Amit Shah, who assured all necessary assistance.Over 300 roads were temporarily closed, more than 200 state-run bus trips were affected, and power outages were reported in many areas.The IMD has issued a red alert for Valsad, Navsari, Dangs, Surat and Tapi for Thursday and Friday. NDRF and SDRF teams are deployed across the affected districts, with additional teams on standby in Amreli, Junagadh and Bhavnagar.Several trains to Mumbai were cancelled or short-terminated due to rising water levels along rail routes in south Gujarat.The state has received around 36 per cent of its annual average rainfall so far, with south Gujarat recording over 62 per cent of its seasonal average.
Article Summary: Valsad district was the worst hit, with many talukas receiving over 600 mm of rain during the same period. A red alert for heavy to extremely heavy rainfall was in effect for Valsad, Navsari, Surat and Tapi districts. Indian Air Force and Coast Guard put on alert for rescue efforts.

Article Title: ‘Must be handled with empathy’: BJP veteran Murli Manohar Joshi breaks ranks, backs NEET protesters
Article Text:
File photo: Veteran BJP leader Murli Manohar Joshi (picture credit: PTI)

BJP, opposition continue war of words

Opposition backs students, Parliament remains deadlocked

NEW DELHI: Veteran BJP leader and former Union minister Murli Manohar Joshi on Thursday extended support to students protesting over the alleged NEET paper leak, saying their concerns about the examination system are "genuine" and should be addressed with empathy rather than force.In a rare intervention from a senior BJP figure, Joshi also criticised the police action against protesting students in Delhi earlier this week, describing it as "merciless" and warning that such use of force could alienate large sections of society from the government's vision of a Viksit Bharat."It is painful to see young students from different parts of the country assembled for days on the streets of New Delhi. Their anxieties and concerns regarding the examination system are genuine and must be handled with empathy and a desire to find a lasting solution," Joshi said in a post on X."It is very painful to see that even young girls were brutally mishandled," he added.Calling for a more humane approach, Joshi said, "I fervently hope that this is not treated merely as a law-and-order issue to be tackled through the use of force."He further warned that such action "shall alienate large sections of Indian society from the national goal of Viksit Bharat."Joshi's remarks came amid an intensifying political confrontation over the alleged NEET paper leak and the ongoing Monsoon Session of Parliament.The BJP accused the Congress of avoiding a discussion on the issue in Parliament despite the government expressing readiness for a debate. BJP president Nitin Nabin said the opposition was "running away" from a discussion because its "double standards" would be exposed."The NDA government is fully prepared to hold discussions at every level on the future of students and youngsters," Nabin said, while challenging the opposition to participate in a debate on NEET and other issues concerning students.Finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman also asserted that the Centre had already taken action against those accused in the alleged paper leak and remained committed to ensuring justice for students. She accused the Congress of pursuing "disruptive politics" instead of participating in a parliamentary discussion.Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal appealed to the opposition not to politicise the issue, saying the government would take the "strictest possible action" against those found guilty in paper leak cases.Earlier in the day, Prime Minister Modi issued his first public response to the ongoing agitation, announcing that the government would set up fast-track courts to ensure swift punishment for those involved in paper leak cases."Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth! We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks. Have directed the concerned authorities and officials to take all necessary steps. Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared," PM Modi wrote on X.Meanwhile, opposition parties have continued to press for the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and have linked the issue to the alleged assault on student protesters.Leader of opposition Rahul Gandhi said INDIA bloc leaders were heading to Gandhi Smriti to express solidarity with students affected by the alleged paper leak and those injured during police action."India's students are not alone. The entire Opposition stands with them and their demands," Gandhi wrote on X.The Opposition has made Pradhan's resignation and a discussion under Rule 267 in the Rajya Sabha key demands before agreeing to a debate, while the government has maintained that it is willing to discuss the issue without preconditions.The deadlock has repeatedly disrupted proceedings in both Houses of Parliament.
Article Summary: BJP leader Murli Manohar Joshi extends support to students protesting over alleged NEET paper leak. Joshi says concerns are "genuine" and should be addressed with empathy rather than force. He also criticises police action against protesting students in Delhi as "merciless"

Article Title: 'We are with you': Rahul Gandhi says INDIA bloc stopped during march in support of protesting students
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi leads INDIA bloc leaders to Gandhi Smriti amid heavy barricading

INDIA bloc heads to Gandhi Smriti in support of students

Heavy police deployment outside Rahul Gandhi's residence

Opposition leaders meet amid Parliament deadlock

NEW DELHI: Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi on Thursday said Opposition MPs took to the streets in solidarity with protesting students over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak, alleging that they were stopped by authorities while attempting to march to India Gate and later Gandhi Smriti.Speaking to reporters after Opposition leaders were halted, Congress MP said the message of the protest was to assure students that the INDIA bloc stood firmly with them."Our students are on the road, so all the Opposition MPs felt that we should also be on the road. All the Opposition MPs wanted to go to India Gate. They refused, stopped us. Then we said that we want to go Tees January (Gandhi Smriti). They have put the bus here and left it. We are not able to go ahead but the main message is to the students. We are with you," he said.Earlier, Rahul Gandhi announced that INDIA bloc leaders would travel to Gandhi Smriti on Tees January Marg to express solidarity with students affected by the NEET-UG controversy and those injured during recent protests.In a post on X, Rahul said the visit was also meant to remember students who had allegedly died by suicide following the paper leak controversy."Right now, MPs and leaders of the INDIA alliance are peacefully heading to Gandhi Smriti on Tees January Marg. We go to remember the students we lost - the children driven to take their own lives after the NEET paper leak. And we go to stand with the students who lie injured today - beaten for peacefully demanding justice and accountability. India's students are not alone. The entire Opposition stands with them and their demands," he wrote.Opposition MPs boarded a bus from Rahul's residence for the proposed visit.Police barricades and a heavy security presence were deployed outside Rahul Gandhi's residence on Sunehri Bagh Road before the Opposition leaders left.Rapid Action Force buses were also stationed in the area.Congress workers gathered outside the residence, with some allegedly breaching security arrangements, following which police appealed to them to end the protest.Indian Youth Congress president Uday Bhanu Chib alleged that Rahul Gandhi had been placed under "house arrest" to prevent him from joining the demonstration."There can be no greater misfortune for the country's politics and democracy than this. We were going towards India Gate, and the country's Leader of the Opposition, Rahul Gandhi, was going to raise his voice for the students of the country. But fearing this, Narendra Modi and Amit Shah have placed him under house arrest today," Chib claimed.Congress MP Renuka Chowdhury said the Opposition would not be intimidated."Wherever we go, this country is ours. These people are intimidating us by calling the police. We are not afraid of this," she said.Congress MP Rajeev Shukla said, "We are all heading towards Gandhi Smriti...They are scared, so they have put up barricades...".Before leaving for Gandhi Smriti, several INDIA bloc leaders met at Rahul Gandhi's residence amid the continuing deadlock in Parliament over the NEET-UG paper leak issue.Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, Sonia Gandhi, P Chidambaram, KC Venugopal, Jairam Ramesh, Pramod Tiwari, Renuka Chowdhury, NCP (SP) MP Supriya Sule, RJD MP Misa Bharati, TMC leaders Mahua Moitra and suspended MP Kalyan Banerjee, CPI MP P. Sandosh Kumar and Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Arvind Sawant were among those present.The meeting comes as the Opposition continues to demand Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation and a discussion under Rule 267 in the Rajya Sabha over the NEET-UG paper leak.The Centre has maintained that it is willing to discuss the issue but has objected to the Opposition attaching preconditions for the debate.
Article Summary: Opposition MPs allege they were stopped by authorities while attempting to march to India Gate and later Gandhi Smriti. Indian Youth Congress president Uday Bhanu Chib alleged that Rahul Gandhi had been placed under "house arrest" to prevent him from joining the demonstration.

Article Title: Evening news wrap: Parliament deadlock over NEET continues, Gulf invests to find new oil routes & more
Article Text:
Evening news wrap

Government reaches out to opposition for NEET debate without preconditions; opposition insists on Pradhan's resignation.

Gulf nations spend billions on new oil pipelines to bypass Strait of Hormuz as Iran blockade continues.

CJP and government locked in standoff over venue for talks as protest demanding Pradhan's resignation continues.

India's first hydrogen-powered train clocks 900 km in five days of commercial operations on Jind-Sonipat route.

Iran claims its strikes destroyed Patriot systems and THAAD radar at US bases in Jordan and Kuwait; US yet to confirm.

Here are the top 5 stories of the day

Government reaches out to opposition for NEET debate, urges talks without preconditions

Gulf nations spend billions on finding new oil routes to bypass Strait of Hormuz

CJP protest continues as government, activists locked in standoff over venue for talks

Iran claims strikes destroyed Patriot systems, THAAD radar at US bases

India's first hydrogen-powered train clocks 900 km in five days

The government has reached out to opposition parties to break the Parliament deadlock over the NEET paper leak, offering a discussion without preconditions but opposing the demand for education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Union home minister Amit Shah met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla after repeated adjournments, while opposition leaders including Rahul Gandhi and Akhilesh Yadav also held talks with the Speaker. Read full story.

Gulf nations are accelerating pipeline projects worth billions of dollars to create alternative export routes bypassing the Strait of Hormuz, with at least seven major projects under construction, planned or under discussion. Saudi Arabia's East-West pipeline and UAE's Fujairah terminal are operating near full capacity, while Abu Dhabi is speeding up a $3 billion pipeline to increase Fujairah's capacity by 1.2 million barrels a day. Iraq is reviving plans for pipelines through Turkey and Jordan, but analysts warn alternative routes remain vulnerable to Houthi attacks. Read full story. The Cockroach Janata Party and the government remained locked in a standoff over the venue for talks, with the CJP insisting on Jantar Mantar while the government proposed an alternative location.The protest demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET paper leak has entered its second month. Police action against protesters and the detention of CJP leaders have further escalated tensions. Follow top developments. Iran's IRGC claimed it destroyed Patriot air defence systems, a THAAD missile defence radar and an MQ-9 Reaper drone depot in retaliatory strikes on US bases in Jordan and Kuwait, though the claims could not be independently verified and the US military has not confirmed any damage. Read full story. India's first hydrogen fuel cell-powered passenger train has covered around 900 kilometres during the first five days of commercial operations on the Jind-Sonipat route in Haryana, with passenger fares ranging between Rs 10 and Rs 25. Read full story.
Article Summary: Gulf nations spend billions on new oil pipelines to bypass Strait of Hormuz as Iran blockade continues. CJP and government locked in standoff over venue for talks as protest demanding Pradhan's resignation continues. India's first hydrogen-powered train clocks 900 km in five days of commercial operations on Jind-Sonipat route.

Article Title: Trump says Saudi nuclear deal hinges on kingdom joining Abraham Accords with Israel
Article Text:
Poll Vote & Share your view Should Saudi Arabia be required to normalize relations with Israel before receiving nuclear cooperation from the US? Yes, it's a necessary step. No, they should be treated independently. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

US President Donald Trump on Thursday said the proposed US-Saudi civil nuclear agreement would only be approved if Saudi Arabia normalises diplomatic relations with Israel by joining the Abraham Accords, adding that the deal would not permit uranium enrichment or any military nuclear programme.Trump's remarks come a day after the United States and Saudi Arabia signed a civil nuclear cooperation framework, known as a 123 Agreement, that will now require approval from the US Congress.In a post on social media, Trump said, "The Civil Nuclear Deal (There will be no enrichment of material!) being made between the United States Department of Energy and Saudi Arabia, which pertains only to non-military use such as the ones that Iran and UAE (and others) already have, will be approved, but is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords. The United States is not opposed to Civil (Non-Enriched) Nuclear Facilities. "Trump's statement marks a shift from the framework announced on Wednesday, under which the two countries agreed to cooperate on Saudi Arabia's civilian nuclear programme. The agreement, signed by US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Saudi Energy Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman, lays the groundwork for nuclear cooperation while requiring congressional review before it can take effect.The Trump administration has said the agreement adheres to the highest standards of nuclear safety and non-proliferation. Secretary of State Marco Rubio also defended the deal, saying the United States would not enter into any arrangement that increased the risk of nuclear weapons proliferation.The agreement has nevertheless drawn scrutiny from some US lawmakers, who have raised concerns that allowing Saudi Arabia to pursue uranium enrichment for civilian purposes could eventually create pathways for nuclear weapons development.Trump's latest remarks also appear to narrow the scope of the proposed agreement by insisting there would be "no enrichment of material" and linking final approval to Saudi Arabia's recognition of Israel through the Abraham Accords, the US-brokered normalisation agreements signed by Israel with several Arab states during Trump's first term.The announcement comes as the United States and Israel continue military operations against Iran, with Washington maintaining that Tehran's uranium enrichment programme could enable it to develop nuclear weapons, a charge Iran denies, insisting its nuclear activities are solely for peaceful purposes.
Article Summary: US President Donald Trump said the proposed US-Saudi civil nuclear agreement would only be approved if Saudi Arabia normalises diplomatic relations with Israel. Trump's remarks come a day after the United States and Saudi Arabia signed a civil nuclear cooperation framework. The agreement has drawn scrutiny from some US lawmakers who have raised concerns that allowing Saudi Arabia to pursue uranium enrichment for civilian purposes could eventually create pathways for nuclear weapons development.

Article Title: CJP protest: Passports of people involved in violence may be cancelled, says report
Article Text:
Police vows strict action against those involved in violence

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you support the cancellation of passports for those involved in violent protests? Yes, it's justified No, it's too harsh 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion
