NEW DELHI: People found to be directly involved in the violence during the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led protests could face cancellation of their passports, news agency PTI reported citing Delhi Police sources on Thursday.They said investigators are using CCTV footage, video recordings and other technical evidence to identify those who were directly involved in rioting and violent incidents during the protest.The sources added that authorities could initiate proceedings to cancel the passports of individuals found to have actively participated in the violence, in line with the relevant legal provisions.The latest police action follows violence that broke out near Jantar Mantar last night during protests over the NEET paper leak issue.According to Delhi Police, inspector Nand Kishor Singh was injured after he was allegedly attacked by a group of protesters while walking from his parked vehicle to his duty point near Jantar Mantar."There were around 4,000 to 5,000 people gathered there. They were raising anti-police slogans and anti-government slogans. I thought they were students and I was going to perform my duty," PTI quoted Singh saying."They assaulted me with lathis, sticks, stones and whatever sharp objects they had in their hands.I sustained injuries and somehow managed to escape. I was in uniform when they attacked me," he added.The clashes between protesters and police left several police personnel injured, according to the force. Delhi Police has since launched an investigation to identify those allegedly involved in the assault on officers as well as damage to public property, saying action is being taken against those responsible.
Article Summary: People found to be directly involved in the violence during the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led protests could face cancellation of their passports. Police are using CCTV footage, video recordings and other technical evidence to identify those involved in rioting and violent incidents.

Article Title: 'Will fight for them': Rahul, INDIA bloc leaders pay tribute to NEET suicide victims — top developments
Article Text:
'Will fight for them': Rahul, INDIA bloc leaders pay tribute to NEET suicide victims

Here are the top developments:

Centre appoints Naresh Gangwar as new Education Secretary

Delhi HC notifies fast-track court for paper leak cases

17 Delhi Metro stations to remain closed from Friday morning



Sonam Wangchuk urges peaceful protests on Day 26 of hunger strike

Rahul Gandhi along with opposition leaders pay tribute

INDIA bloc leaders reach Gandhi Smriti

Rahul claims INDIA bloc march stopped by police

INDIA bloc heads to Gandhi Smriti





Opposition huddle at Rahul's residence

Mobile internet suspended around Jantar Mantar till midnight

Centre renews offer for talks, CJP rejects venue proposed by govt

CJP demands neutral venue, seeks 'concrete outcome'

Passport action warning

CJP announces nationwide protests on July 24

PM Modi announces fast-track courts for paper leak cases

Anna Hazare begins silent protest in support of students

Parliament remains paralysed over paper leak issue

Centre likely to convene floor leaders' meeting

NEW DELHI: The confrontation over NEET paper leak widened on Thursday, with the opposition taking its protest to the streets even as the Centre and the Cockroach Janta Party ( CJP ) remained locked in a disagreement over fresh talks.The standoff between the Centre and the CJP deepened as both sides disagreed over the venue for fresh talks, even as Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced fast-track courts to ensure speedy punishment in paper leak cases.While the government reiterated that it was ready for dialogue and invited the protesters for talks at Union health minister JP Nadda 's residence or office, the CJP rejected the proposal, insisting that any meeting should either be held at Jantar Mantar or at a mutually agreed neutral venue.The Centre appointed senior IAS officer Naresh Gangwar, a 1994-batch Rajasthan cadre officer, as the new Secretary in the Department of School Education and Literacy, replacing Vineet Joshi as part of a bureaucratic reshuffle.Gangwar, who was serving as Secretary in the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying, has held several senior positions in the Rajasthan government and the Centre.The government did not specify any reason for the change. The appointment comes amid continuing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak and demands for reforms in the examination system.The Delhi high court designated the court of Judicial Officer Anu Grover Baliga at the Rouse Avenue Court Complex as a Specially Designated Fast Track Court to hear cases under the Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024 and related offences with immediate effect.The notification came hours after Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that fast-track courts would be set up to ensure swift trials and stringent punishment in paper leak cases amid ongoing protests over the alleged NEET paper leak.The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) announced that 17 Metro stations will remain closed from 7:30 am on Friday (July 24) until further orders. While entry and exit will not be permitted at these stations, interchange facilities will continue to be available at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House and Central Secretariat.The affected stations include Lok Kalyan Marg, Rajiv Chowk, Patel Chowk, Ramakrishna Ashram Marg, Barakhambha Road, Supreme Court, Seva Teerth, Janpath, Mandi House, Central Secretariat, ITO, Delhi Gate, Indraprastha, Khan Market, Jor Bagh, Shivaji Stadium and Jhandewalan.Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who is on the 26th day of his indefinite hunger strike, appealed to supporters to maintain peace amid the ongoing protests.In a post on X, he said he was pained to learn that 'antisocial elements' were trying to provoke violence elsewhere, even as the demonstrations at Jantar Mantar remained peaceful.'While at Jantar Mantar the protests remain peaceful I’m pained to learn that elsewhere some antisocial elements are taking advantage of the peaceful protests to provoke violence. No matter what the other side does our response must only be flowers. Please keep this tradition at all costs,' he wrote.Rahul Gandhi, along with Congress and other INDIA bloc leaders, paid homage at Gandhi Smriti to students who allegedly died by suicide over NEET examination paper leaks.The opposition said the tribute was dedicated to the memory of the students and vowed to continue its fight for justice for them.Buses carrying INDIA bloc MPs, including Rahul Gandhi arrived at Gandhi Smriti, where opposition leaders will pay tributes to the students who committed suicide over the alleged NEET paper leak.This comes as earlier, opposition leaders were stopped from vising the Gandhi Smriti. "To the memory of those students who are no longer among us Towards Gandhi Smriti," Congress handle wrote in a post on X.In a post on X, Rahul wrote: "No one can stop us from standing with the students of India."After reaching Gandhi Smriti, Rahul said: "Our leader Mahatma Gandhi was assassinated over here. Today, the Modi government is assassinating democracy in India, brutalizing students, and beating students. The Opposition is not going to accept this. We are with the students, and Mr. Narendra Modi has to remove the Education Minister and apologize."Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra said, "What Mahatma Gandhi did, and what our farmers and the poor did, was a truly unique revolution. A truly unique movement because no one was ever harmed. Today, in that very country, you're beating children because they're demanding their rights. So, we all see how much is going wrong, and our country's democracy, for which Gandhiji was martyred, for which so many people were martyred. Today, in this very country, we're seeing democracy is being destroyed... Those who wear uniforms, who stand on your borders. For what? So that their children can be beaten. Why? Because their exam papers are being leaked. They are demanding their rights and justice for this."Rahul Gandhi claimed that he and INDIA bloc MPs were prevented from proceeding to Gandhi Smriti by security personnel after they were denied permission to march to India Gate."Our students are on the road, so all the opposition MPs felt that we should also be on the road. All the opposition MPs wanted to go to India Gate. They refused, stopped us. Then we said that we want to go Tees January( Gandhi Smriti). They have put the bus here and left it. We are not able to go ahead but the main message is to the students. We are with you."The Congress MP also claimed that the bus carrying them to Gandhi Smriti was parked, after which the driver got off and left it there.Congress MP Renuka Chowdhury said, "Why should we explain this to such a stubborn government? Have they ever gone there (Gandhi Smriti)?... They (government) do not have the courage to come out and talk.""This is the power of Gandhi. This is Gandhi's country. We are marching peacefully to Gandhi Smriti to raise our voices for the youth. Who could object to that?" Congress leader Supriya Srinate said.After the INDIA bloc meeting concluded, Rahul Gandhi said alliance MPs and leaders were heading to Gandhi Smriti on Tees January Marg to pay tribute to students who died by suicide following the NEET paper leak and to express solidarity with those injured during the recent protests.Rahul along with several other opposition MPs boarded the bus to leave for Gandhi Smriti from his residence.In a post on X, Gandhi wrote, "Right now, MPs and leaders of the INDIA alliance are peacefully heading to Gandhi Smriti on Tees January Marg. We go to remember the students we lost - the children driven to take their own lives after the NEET paper leak. And we go to stand with the students who lie injured today - beaten for peacefully demanding justice and accountability. India’s students are not alone. The entire Opposition stands with them and their demands. "Meanwhile, security was also tightened outside Dharmendra Pradhan's residence.Several INDIA bloc leaders arrived at Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi's residence for a meeting amid the ongoing row over the NEET paper leak and student protests.As senior leaders gathered, police presence outside Rahul Gandhi's residence was stepped up. Venugopal alleged that Delhi had been turned into a "war-like situation", while Kharge quipped that he had been invited for "tea"."Sahab invited for tea. That's why I have come to have tea," Kharge said.Congress leader P Chidambaram, Sonia Gandhi, TMC MPs Kalyan Banerjee and Mahua Moitra and Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Arvind Sawant also arrived for the meeting.A bus carrying Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel also arrived outside the residence of Congress MP Rahul Gandhi in Delhi amid the ongoing protests.Congress MP Amrinder Singh Raja Warring after arriving at the residence of Rahul Gandhi said, "If we reveal our plan, the enemy is very alert. Delhi Police, Amit Shah ji's people, have come here already. We will fight, and we will win."RJD MP Manoj Jha after arriving at Rahul's residence said, "Look at what is happening at Jantar Mantar and in different states. There is so much unrest among the youth...In the coming moments, you will know what we are doing...I am worried after seeing the condition of the country."The Union home ministry extended the suspension of mobile internet services within a 1.5-km radius of Jantar Mantar till midnight, citing "public safety" and the need to avert a public emergency.The restriction comes as the CJP continues its protest demanding education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the alleged NEET paper leak. This is the fifth such suspension order issued since July 17.The Centre once again invited the protesting students for dialogue after an earlier meeting between Union health minister JP Nadda and CJP representatives failed to break the deadlock.Union minister Jitendra Singh said the government had made multiple offers for talks over the past 24 hours and was willing to discuss all issues related to the protests."We are ready to discuss the issue on which the agitation has happened, and all the other related issues as well. The talks can be held with an open mind at the residence of Union health minister JP Nadda... The talks could be held at Nadda's residence or at the office," Singh said.JP Nadda also reiterated that the government remained open to discussions.Responding to the government's offer, CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka said the protesters had no objection to talks but would not attend a meeting at the residence of a Union minister.He said the CJP had earlier suggested that both sides agree on a neutral venue after the government conveyed its reluctance to visit Jantar Mantar."Yesterday, they told us, 'Please don't ask us to come to Jantar Mantar. It will create problems for us because people are very angry.' So we said, 'Alright, neither your place nor ours. Let's find a neutral venue where we can meet.' Since they seem reluctant to come to Jantar Mantar, we suggested that they recommend any neutral location that is neither their residence nor Jantar Mantar."Ranka said the protesters had rejected the proposal to meet at JP Nadda's residence or office."Even now, Union minister Jitendra Singh is saying that the meeting can take place either at JP Nadda's office or his residence. But the people have rejected that proposal. If a meeting is to take place, it will not be held at Nadda's residence. It should either be at Jantar Mantar or at some other neutral venue."He also said the protesters wanted meaningful negotiations instead of assurances."We have also placed a second condition before the government. This time, we don't want empty assurances. If you are holding talks with us, there has to be something concrete... If you are inviting us for talks, we expect some concrete outcome."CJP spokesperson Saurav Das said, "Union Minister Jitendra Singh said that we can hold talks anywhere; then we have already made our suggestion. Come to Jantar Mantar or to a neutral venue like the Constitution Club of India. We will meet there. I have not received any message from their side yet...They should contact us directly and discuss where to meet, when to meet, and how long the discussion will continue."Delhi Police said that people found to be directly involved in the violence during the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) protest could face cancellation of their passports.According to PTI, the move comes after clashes near Jantar Mantar left several police personnel injured. Police said investigations are underway to identify those involved in the assault on officers and damage to public property.The Cockroach Janta Party announced that it would organise nationwide protests on July 24 as its agitation entered another phase.The organisation maintained that the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan remained its primary demand and said the movement would continue until the government accepted its key demands, including accountability for the alleged paper leak and withdrawal of FIRs against peaceful protesters.Amid the continuing protests, Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced that the Centre would establish fast-track courts to ensure swift trials and stringent punishment for those involved in examination paper leaks.In a post on X, PM Modi said the government had directed authorities to take all necessary steps to safeguard students' interests."Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth. We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks... Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared," he said.Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, however, criticised the announcement, accusing the government of failing to protect the education system and reiterating the Opposition's demand for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.Veteran social activist Anna Hazare on Thursday began a maun andolan (silent protest) in Maharashtra's Ahilyanagar in support of students protesting over the alleged examination irregularities.Hazare had earlier backed the student movement and urged the government to address concerns over alleged paper leaks and restore public confidence in the examination system.His protest comes as demonstrations continue at Delhi's Jantar Mantar despite heightened security and repeated clashes between protesters and police.Parliament witnessed another day of disruptions as both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha were adjourned repeatedly amid protests by Opposition MPs demanding the resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.The government maintained that it was ready to hold a discussion on the issue without any preconditions, while the Opposition insisted that Pradhan must resign before any debate could take place.Parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju said the government was prepared to hold discussions for any duration the Opposition wanted, but accused opposition parties of avoiding a debate by insisting on resignation first.Government sources said the Centre is likely to convene a meeting of floor leaders to finalise a discussion on the paper leak issue in Parliament.According to the sources, the government has urged opposition parties to participate in the debate without linking it to the resignation of the education minister.The move comes as the Centre continues efforts to break both the parliamentary deadlock and the impasse with the CJP over the ongoing protests.
Article Summary: 17 Delhi Metro stations will remain closed from 7:30 am on Friday (July 24) until further orders. Delhi high court designated the court of Judicial Officer Anu Grover Baliga at the Rouse Avenue Court Complex as a Specially Designated Fast Track Court to hear cases under the Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024 and related offences. Naresh Gangwar, a 1994-batch Rajasthan cadre officer, was appointed as the new Secretary in the Department of School Education and Literacy.

Article Title: US strikes Iran for 13th successive night; Trump says Tehran will pay for ship damage
Article Text:
US-Iran hostilities stretch into 13th night

'All damages will be paid for by Iranian money,' says Trump

Iran targets US bases in Gulf

Two weeks after an interim truce effectively collapsed, bringing renewed escalation to the conflict, the United States launched overnight airstrikes on Iran into Thursday, prompting Tehran to retaliate with missile attacks targeting neighbouring Arab countries hosting US military bases.The US carried out another wave of missile strikes late Thursday and early Friday, marking the 13th consecutive night of attacks.Iranian state media reported that missiles struck Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz on both nights.The strategic waterway, now virtually closed to commercial shipping, has emerged as the focal point of the conflict, which is well into its fifth month and has already claimed thousands of lives while fuelling fears of a global economic slowdown.US president Donald Trump on Thursday said any damage to ships and cargo resulting from the ongoing conflict with Iran would be paid for using Iranian assets held under Washington's control."Please let this statement serve to represent, until further notice, that from this point forth, any and all damages done to Ships, Cargo, or anything related thereto, will be paid for by Iranian Money that the United States has in its possession, and controls.These damages may be very substantial but, nevertheless, this is the fair and equitable thing to do. Thank you for your attention to this matter!" Trump said in a post on Truth Social.The latest Truth Social post came hours after Trump on Thursday said that Iran cannot be allowed to possess nuclear weapons, adding that Washington was doing "extremely well" as the conflict between the two countries continued.Iran said it targeted US missile systems, weapons depots and fuel storage facilities in Jordan, along with American military positions in Kuwait, in its latest round of retaliatory strikes.Jordan's military said it intercepted four Iranian missiles and six drones over the past 24 hours, adding that all but one missile were successfully brought down. The remaining missile landed in an uninhabited area, causing no reported casualties.Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed they struck a US electronic warfare unit at the Al-Adiri base in Kuwait, saying the attack caused unspecified casualties. The strike, they said, was in retaliation for overnight US attacks on Iran's Shalamcheh border crossing with Iraq.Iranian military spokesperson Brigadier General Mohammad Akraminia said Tehran would continue its retaliatory operations as long as the United States persisted with attacks on Iranian infrastructure and coastal regions, according to Iranian state television.However, neither the United States nor Kuwaiti authorities have reported any casualties or damage at the base.
Article Summary: US carried out another wave of missile strikes late Thursday and early Friday, marking the 13th consecutive night of attacks. Iran said it targeted US missile systems, weapons depots and fuel storage facilities in Jordan, along with American military positions in Kuwait. US president Donald Trump said any damage to ships and cargo resulting from the ongoing conflict with Iran would be paid for using Iranian assets.

Article Title: Watch: Suspect yelling 'Allahu Akbar' stabs two in New York, arrested
Article Text: NEW YORK: A 51-year-old man allegedly shouted "Allahu Akbar" before stabbing two men in separate attacks on New York City's Upper West Side on Thursday, prompting New York Police Department (NYPD) to investigate the incidents as potential hate crimes.According to Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch, Raul Morales, 51, first attacked a 57-year-old Asian man near Central Park West, stabbing him in the back with a knife around 1.30 pm. Minutes later, he allegedly stabbed a 50-year-old 'identifiable Jewish' man, who was wearing a yarmulke and leaving a synagogue, with a screwdriver in the torso.Both victims were taken to Mount Sinai Morningside (formerly St. Luke's) Hospital and were reported to be in stable condition.Police arrested Morales about an hour later after he allegedly barricaded himself inside a nearby NYCHA apartment. Officers recovered both a knife and a screwdriver during the arrest.Tisch said victim and witness statements indicated Morales repeatedly yelled "Allahu Akbar" during the attacks."Detectives are continuing to work the case and determine motive... The NYPD is currently evaluating whether this is a potential hate crime," she said, adding that mental health may also have been a factor in the attacks.New York Governor Kathy Hochul condemned what she described as an apparent attack on Jewish New Yorkers, New York Post cites. She said, "Anyone who tries to tear that apart will be met with the full force of the law."Mayor Zohran Mamdani said he had been briefed on the "horrifying stabbings" and thanked officers for their swift response. "The NYPD’s initial investigation suggests that mental health may have been a factor. They are also evaluating these stabbings as potential hate crimes. These hateful and despicable attacks have no place in our city," he wrote.
Article Summary: A 51-year-old man allegedly shouted "Allahu Akbar" before stabbing two men in separate attacks on New York City's Upper West Side. New York Police Department (NYPD) to investigate the incidents as potential hate crimes. Governor Kathy Hochul condemned what she described as an apparent attack on Jewish New Yorkers.

Article Title: Celeste Rivas' clothing presented as new evidence in D4vd preliminary hearing
Article Text:
Getty Images

Celeste Rivas' clothing becomes key evidence in D4vd preliminary hearing

Court hears more testimony about Tesla, garage, and D4vd's earnings

New evidence was presented in court on Thursday during the preliminary hearing of singer D4vd, whose real name is David Burke. Prosecutors called LAPD criminalist Lisa Lahendro to explain the condition of the clothing found with Celeste Rivas' body. The courtroom also heard more testimony from forensic experts about evidence collected from Burke's Tesla and Hollywood Hills home. The hearing followed two days of emotional testimony, with Rivas' family watching as investigators described the evidence connected to the case. Burke has denied the charges against him.LAPD criminalist Lisa Lahendro told the court that the clothing found with Celeste Rivas' body included a Victoria's Secret thong, a Forever 21 tube top, and leggings.She said the clothes were in very poor condition. According to her testimony, they were heavily soiled, very wet, covered with many insects, and had a very strong smell.Lahendro also testified that detectives later found pieces of black fabric inside a trash bag in David Burke's garage. Investigators said the fabric was compared with Rivas' clothing as part of the investigation.Earlier in the week, the court was shown disturbing photographs of Rivas' body.Her parents were present in the courtroom as investigators described the evidence. Detectives testified that Rivas' body had been found in separate pieces, and several photographs were shown during the hearing.The hearing also included testimony from other LAPD forensic experts.Criminalist Chelsea Murillo said she examined Burke's Tesla after Rivas' body had been removed. She told the court that the car had a very strong smell that reminded her of a decomposing body. Murillo also said she found at least 11 air fresheners inside the vehicle, including some in the trunk and center console.Another criminalist, Lauren Wallace, testified that she searched Burke's Hollywood Hills home for evidence. She said tests found signs of possible blood on the brick floor underneath mats in the garage.The court also heard testimony about Burke's finances. Lewis Poimiroo, senior vice president of finance at Interscope Records, said Burke earned about $2.77 million in 2025. The testimony showed that he continued to receive money from his music career in the months after prosecutors say Rivas was killed.Burke has been charged with first-degree murder with special circumstances, mutilation of human remains, and lewd and lascivious acts involving a child under the age of 14. Prosecutors also allege that he arranged an Uber to bring Rivas from her family's home in Lake Elsinore to his Hollywood Hills home on April 23, 2025.As the preliminary hearing continues, prosecutors are presenting forensic evidence and witness testimony to support their case. The court will later decide whether there is enough evidence for the case to move forward to trial.
Article Summary: Court hears more testimony about Tesla, garage, and D4vd's earnings. Prosecutors called LAPD criminalist Lisa Lahendro to explain the condition of the clothing found with Celeste Rivas' body. The courtroom also heard more testimony from forensic experts about evidence collected from Burke's Tesla.

Article Title: 'Gentle giant': US mourns Sgt Angel Rampersad killed in Iranian attack
Article Text:
Sgt. Angel S. Rampersad(X)

US Army Sgt Angel Rampersad, the 28-year-old soldier believed to be among those killed in last week's Iranian attack on a military base in Jordan, was mourned by family, friends and neighbours in New York's Queens on Wednesday. American flags flew at half-staff as neighbours mourned.A flag-draped transfer case carrying Rampersad's remains arrived at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware, where her mother, Carol Acevedo, attended the military's dignified transfer ceremony. The soldier had initially been listed as missing before military officials confirmed her death.According to US Department Of War press release, she is believed to have been killed in action on July 17, 2026, during an enemy attack at Muwaffaq Salti Air Base, Jordan. She was assigned to 1st Battalion, 57th Air Defense Artillery Regiment, 52nd Air Defense Artillery Brigade, 10th Army Air & Missile Defense Command, Ansbach, Germany.Born in Trinidad, Rampersad moved to New York at the age of five. She attended John Jay College of Criminal Justice before joining the Army in 2019 and re-enlisted last year. Friends said an uncle's military service inspired her decision to enlist.Neighbours remembered Rampersad as warm, humble and deeply committed to serving others.In a statement shared through a family friend, her mother described her as a "gentle giant" who was "a defender and a friend."Rampersad's death has drawn tributes from New York leaders, including Governor Kathy Hochul, who called her life "one of courage and selflessness," and Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who said her sacrifice reflected how wars overseas deeply affect families back home."Sgt. Rampersad's courage and sacrifice will be remembered not just in Queens, but across all five boroughs. My deepest condolences are with her family, loved ones, and all those grieving this profound loss. For so many New Yorkers, wars overseas are never distant—they are felt here at home. The sacrifices made by service members and their families are a constant reminder of this," Mamdani wrote on X.
Article Summary: US Army Sgt Angel Rampersad, 28, believed to be among those killed in last week's Iranian attack on a military base in Jordan. American flags flew at half- staff as neighbours mourned in New York's Queens. Her mother described her as a "gentle giant" who was "a defender and a friend"

Article Title: Indian-origin woman held in Canada for $60,000 rental scam
Article Text:
Savreet Kaur

An Indian-origin woman has been arrested in Canada after police uncovered a rental scam that left dozens of prospective tenants without homes and out of more than $60,000, CTV News reported.According to Peel Regional Police, the accused allegedly advertised rental properties, including basement apartments, on social media between May 2025 and February 2026. People who responded to the listings signed tenancy agreements and paid deposits covering the first and last month's rent.Police said the fraud came to light only when tenants arrived at the advertised addresses with their belongings, only to discover the properties were unavailable. In several cases, victims had already moved out of their previous homes before realising they had been deceived.Police have identified 17 cases linked to the alleged rental fraud, with total losses exceeding C$60,000. "None of the victims received the promised accommodations or had their money returned," Peel Regional Police said.Savreet Kaur, 27, of Brampton, was arrested on July 13. She faces 38 counts of fraud over C$5,000 and one count of obstructing police. The allegations have not been proven in court. She was held in custody pending a bail hearing.
Article Summary: Savreet Kaur, 27, of Brampton, arrested on July 13. She faces 38 counts of fraud over C$5,000 and one count of obstructing police. Police have identified 17 cases linked to the alleged rental fraud, with total losses exceeding C$60,000.

Article Title: China's Moonshot AI 'stole' from Anthropic's LLM model, alleges US
Article Text:
OpenAI and Anthropic had already been working with the US government to test unreleased AI models

(This is a Reuters story)

WASHINGTON: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic's most sophisticated AI large language model, Fable, to help create its latest K3 release and has acquired advanced Nvidia AI chips, top Trump administration tech official Michael Kratsios said Wednesday. Large-scale, covert industrial distillation aimed at stealing proprietary US technology and undermining research is unacceptable," Kratsios said.US treasury secretary Scott Bessent said Wednesday in a separate post on X that he was considering adding Moonshot AI to a trade blacklist and imposing sanctions. The Trump administration's accusations will likely ramp up tensions over technology issues between Beijing and Washington .Spokesperson Liu Chang of the Chinese embassy in Washington called Kratsios' comments "entirely unfounded," adding that China respects intellectual property protections. "Relevant individuals in the US should respect the facts, discard prejudice and stop smearing and discrediting China's achievements in the development of its AI industry," he said.
Article Summary: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic's most sophisticated AI large language model, Fable, to help create its latest K3 release. The Trump administration's accusations will likely ramp up tensions over technology issues between Beijing and Washington. Spokesperson Liu Chang of the Chinese embassy in Washington called Kratsios' comments "entirely unfounded"

Article Title: 'Ban Muslims from office': Laura Loomer calls Zohran Mamdani 'national security risk'
Article Text:
Zohran Mamdani and Laura Loomer (R)

America's far-right activist and Donald Trump loyalist Laura Loomer on Wednesday launched a scathing attack on New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani after he called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu as a "war criminal" and said he was "not welcome in New York City."Loomer, who is Jewish, called for a ban on Muslims holding public office in the United States and urged President Donald Trump to reinstate a "Muslim ban.""Ban Muslims from holding office in America," Loomer said.She also appealed directly to MAGA supremo, writing: "We are never going to Make America Safe Again until President Trump goes back to implementing his Muslim ban. This time, make it a Muslim Ban as it was intended to be. Not an Islamic travel ban."Taking to X, Loomer accused Mamdani of sympathising with Iran and Hamas, and claimed, without providing evidence, that he posed a national security risk. "When you allow Muslims to participate in the American political system, you get Twelver Shia Iran regime sympathizers and Hamas lovers like Mamdani governing the largest city in America. It won’t end well."She further claimed that Mamdani would never receive FBI security clearance, saying, "I’m just glad the Trump administration confirmed to me that Mamdani will never be given an FBI clearance."Citing an unnamed source, Loomer wrote, "My senior FBI source told me, 'Senior FBI leadership has reason to believe Mamdani may be having direct communication with Islamic terrorist organizations. He is certainly a national security risk which is why we declined giving him an FBI approved security clearance. He won’t be getting one.'"Loomer added, "I pushed very hard for that to be the case even before Mamdani was elected," and further claimed, "Shocking my FBI source confirmed to me that they allegedly have reason to believe Mamdani is having direct communication with terror orgs."The remarks came after Mamdani intensified his criticism of Netanyahu in a video posted on X on Tuesday, saying the Israeli leader was "not welcome in New York City" and urging the US federal government to enforce the International Criminal Court's (ICC) arrest warrant against him.Calling Netanyahu "a war criminal," Mamdani accused him of committing atrocities during Israel's military campaign in Gaza."He is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people," Mamdani said.He further alleged that Netanyahu was responsible "for the maiming of tens of thousands of children as those who survive undergo amputation without anaesthesia. For the targeting of neonatal hospitals and maternity care centres, denying newborns even the chance to live. For the countless people he starved as he blocked food and humanitarian aid from reaching them."Referring to the ICC arrest warrant, Mamdani said: "There is a reason the International Criminal Court issued a warrant for his arrest. As I've said, I agree with the ICC that Benjamin Netanyahu should be arrested and tried for his crimes as I do for anyone else charged by the ICC."
Article Summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu as a "war criminal" and said he was "not welcome in New York City" Loomer, who is Jewish, called for a ban on Muslims holding public office in the United States and urged President Donald Trump to reinstate a "Muslim ban"

Article Title: US House approves $95 billion Iran war budget, sends Trump-backed plan to Senate
Article Text:
US-Iran war continues (ANI image)

Senate hurdles remain as Republicans differ on funding, voting rules

The US House of Representatives has narrowly approved a $95 billion budget blueprint to fund the war with Iran and advance President Donald Trump's election-related priorities, overcoming resistance within Republican ranks.The measure passed by a 216-214 vote and now heads to the Senate, where its prospects remain uncertain.The proposal would pave the way for a filibuster-proof reconciliation bill, allowing Republicans to bypass Democratic opposition. It includes up to $73 billion in additional military funding, $12 billion for farmers, and $10 billion to implement stricter voting rules sought by Trump.Despite concerns over the cost, scope and political fallout of funding an unpopular war ahead of the midterm elections, all but two House Republicans backed the plan. Republican Representatives Thomas Massie and Warren Davidson, along with Independent Kevin Kiley, voted against it.The budget now faces a tougher path in the Senate, where Republican leaders have expressed reservations. Senate Majority leader John Thune indicated the chamber may delay taking up the House measure until lawmakers address government funding ahead of the September shutdown deadline.Several Republican senators have also raised objections.Armed Services Committee chairman Roger Wicker said the proposal does not provide enough funding for the Pentagon, while Senators Lisa Murkowski and Thom Tillis oppose the inclusion of the SAVE Act, which seeks to tighten voter registration requirements by mandating proof of citizenship, stricter voter identification and curbs on mail-in voting.Murkowski also questioned whether approving supplemental funding could be interpreted by the Trump administration as congressional authorisation for the war with Iran, noting that the president has not sought formal approval from Congress for the conflict.Meanwhile, House Democratic leader Hakeem Jeffries slammed the proposal, calling it an "America Last Republican budget" that would pour billions more into "Operation Epic Fury" in the Middle East, which he described as "a complete and total disaster for the American people."If approved by the Senate, it would mark the third major reconciliation package passed by Republicans in the past 18 months.
Article Summary: US House of Representatives approves $95 billion budget blueprint to fund the war with Iran. Measure passed by a 216-214 vote and now heads to the Senate, where its prospects remain uncertain. Senate Majority leader John Thune indicated the chamber may delay taking up the House measure until lawmakers address government funding ahead of the September shutdown deadline.

Article Title: FBI nabs Indian national Harmanveer Singh over Canada-based gang's drug trafficking racket
Article Text:
Harmanveer Singh (Picture from FBI X handle)

The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) has arrested Indian national Harmanveer Singh for his alleged role in a transnational drug trafficking network linked to the Canada-based organised crime group led by Ravinder Dhanda.Singh was taken into custody in California's Stockton, on Tuesday by officers of the California Highway Patrol, the FBI said on Wednesday.His arrest comes as part of "Operation Hard Ball", a coordinated international law enforcement operation targeting organised crime groups led by Lawrence Bishnoi, Jaggu Bhagwanpuria and Ravinder Dhanda.According to the FBI, Singh is wanted for his alleged involvement in a transnational criminal organisation accused of conspiracy to distribute and possess with intent to distribute controlled substances, as well as conspiracy to export controlled substances.The agency said the Ravinder Dhanda Organised Crime Group, based in Vancouver, Canada, allegedly smuggled large quantities of cocaine and methamphetamine for drug trafficking organisations operating across the United States, Canada and Mexico.On June 23, 2026, the US district court for the central district of California in Los Angeles issued a federal arrest warrant against Singh after he was charged with conspiracy to distribute and possess with intent to distribute controlled substances, conspiracy to export controlled substances, and possession with intent to distribute controlled substances.Earlier this month, law enforcement agencies from the United States, Canada and Europe arrested 24 people, including 11 in California, in a joint operation targeting three India-linked transnational organised crime groups accused of multiple criminal offences.In total, the US department of justice charged 37 individuals under "Operation Hard Ball".The broader operation, spanning the US, Canada and Europe, resulted in three federal indictments against 37 defendants and the arrest of 24 suspects while also targeting other major organised crime syndicates.As part of the same crackdown, federal agents on Thursday arrested 26-year-old Indian national Nitish Kaushal in Vermont as he allegedly attempted to cross into Canada. Authorities said Kaushal is linked to the Jaggu Bhagwanpuria organised crime group. His arrest came two days after the FBI released a wanted poster for him.US authorities have also announced a reward of up to $50,000 for information leading to the arrest of Satinderjeet Singh, alias Goldy Brar, described as "the North American leader of the Lawrence Bishnoi gang".Brar and Lawrence Bishnoi are accused of directing international criminal operations, including allegedly ordering the June 2023 killing of Hardeep Singh Nijjar in British Columbia.
Article Summary: Indian national Harmanveer Singh arrested for alleged role in transnational drug trafficking network. FBI says Singh is linked to Canada-based organised crime group led by Ravinder Dhanda. Arrest comes as part of "Operation Hard Ball", a coordinated international law enforcement operation targeting organised crime groups.

Article Title: 'If we die, we die together': Meet Svetlana Grković who grabbed husband's leg after his body went outside a plane mid-air
Article Text:
Wife grabs husband's legs after his upperbody hangs out of plane mid-air as the window crashed.

Svetlana Grković was flying from Greece to Germany when a window shattered, sucking out the man sitting nearby. Half of his body was outside the window, though he had his seatbelt on. But Ljubisa Karović's wife held him by the legs and said: "If we die, we die together". The incident took place a few days ago on Ryanair flight FR1879, which was flying from Thessaloniki in Greece to Memmingen near Munich in Germany on Friday. It was operated by the budget airline’s subsidiary Malta Air.The flight was airborne for just over an hour and reached 16,000 ft before coming back to Thessaloniki airport.For two minutes, the husband was outside up to his chest.Grković said that with the help of two passengers, she was able to pull her husband, who lost consciousness three times, back inside the aircraft. She said that many people fled their seats to other parts of the plane for safety as the cabin decompressed.Passengers have told local media that Karović had kept his seatbelt on and helped those on board keep hold of him while his upper torso was in the plane’s slipstream outside.The shock of the incident is massive. The husband is in a hospital receiving treatment. “It’s important to me that he’s alive,” the wife said. “His hand is particularly badly injured, and he’s got burns. He’s not able to communicate; he doesn’t remember the whole event.”“Some people came to my aid, I remember one man and one woman,” she added. “That man helped me a lot, Ljubisa and me. I think he was Albanian, thank you very much. I didn’t remember his name, I don’t even know if he told me. I would like to meet him, to thank him personally again.”A Ryanair spokesperson said: “A Ryanair flight from Thessaloniki to Memmingen on Friday morning returned to Thessaloniki shortly after takeoff when a passenger window dislodged inflight. The aircraft landed normally and passengers returned to the terminal. One passenger requested and received medical assistance on the ground in Thessaloniki. In order to minimise any delay, a replacement aircraft was arranged to bring passengers to Memmingen. "
Article Summary: Ljubisa Karović's wife held him by the legs and said: "If we die, we die together". The incident took place on Ryanair flight FR1879, which was flying from Thessaloniki in Greece to Memmingen near Munich in Germany.

Article Title: Larry Ellison’s Oracle wins Pentagon’s ‘first-ever direct award contract’ for $7 billion
Article Text:
Oracle-Pentagon agreement will initially span for five years

Here’s what Pentagon said in its official announcement

Today, the Department of War (DoW) announced a historic up to 10-year, near $7 billion Enterprise Software Agreement (ESA) with Oracle, further advancing the Arsenal of Freedom. Negotiated by the Department of the Navy, this first-ever, direct award contract with Oracle for the Department's on-premises Oracle usage serves to consolidate licensing and will improve visibility of enterprise usage and spend in support of optimizing the Department's technology budgets. By consolidating fragmented, one-off information technology purchases into a unified, single award contract, the agreement is projected to deliver at least $441 million in savings to U.S. taxpayers over its lifecycle.

"By fundamentally improving how we procure on-premises Oracle capabilities, we are driving at least $441 million in taxpayer savings while rapidly and effectively serving our warfighters," said Honorable Kirsten A. Davies, Chief Information Officer for the Department of War. "This nearly $7 billion agreement with Oracle strengthens our digital ecosystem, supporting our warfighters with secure, scalable technology to dominate current and future missions."

"For the Department of the Navy, this agreement supports faster delivery of secure, scalable software from the shore enterprise to the tactical edge for our Navy and Marine Corps teams," said Barry Tanner, Performing the Duties of the Department of the Navy Chief Information Officer. "Reducing fragmented procurement and advancing enterprise standardization will strengthen interoperability, reduce cybersecurity risk, and help us focus resources on resilient capabilities that can scale, integrate, and endure in contested conditions."

Structured with a five-year base period and a five-year option period, this ESA supports the entire DoW, as well as the Coast Guard and the Intelligence Community.

US Department of War has announced a nearly $7 billion Enterprise Software Agreement (ESA) with Trump supporter Larry Ellison’s Oracle for up to 10 years. The agreement is aimed to consolidate the department’s on-premise software licenses into a single contract. “Negotiated by the Department of the Navy, this first-ever, direct award contract with Oracle for the Department's on-premises Oracle usage serves to consolidate licensing and will improve visibility of enterprise usage and spend in support of optimizing the Department's technology budgets,” the agency said in an announcement.The Oracle-Pentagon agreement will initially span for five years with a further five-year option. "By fundamentally improving how we procure on-premises Oracle capabilities, we are driving at least $441 million in taxpayer savings while rapidly and effectively serving our warfighters," Pentagon ‌Chief ⁠Information Officer Kirsten Davies said.As mentioned above, Oracle founder, Larry Ellison, has a close relationship with the US President Donald Trump. According to a Wall Street Journal report, Ellison has donated more than $45 million to nonprofits and programs backing Trump.
Article Summary: US Department of War has announced a nearly $7 billion Enterprise Software Agreement (ESA) with Trump supporter Larry Ellison’s Oracle for up to 10 years. The agreement is aimed to consolidate the department's on-premise software licenses into a single contract. The Oracle-Pentagon agreement will initially span for five years with a further five-year option.

Article Title: US government announces new global visa restriction policy; says: United States will go after those who ...
Article Text:
Read Marco Rubio’s letter about new global visa restriction policy



“As President Trump made clear in E.O. 14390 on Combatting Cybercrime, Fraud, and Predatory Schemes Against American Citizens, his administration is taking action to counter an unprecedented threat from online investment scams. Often orchestrated by Chinese transnational criminal organizations, scammers have defrauded U.S. citizens of at least $10 billion in 2024 alone while fueling corruption, money laundering, and human trafficking. American children also have been specifically targeted by offenders operating overseas for sextortion scams, devastating families and futures.

Today, I am announcing a new global visa restriction policy under Section 212(a)(3)(C) of the Immigration and Nationality Act. This policy targets individuals responsible for, or complicit in, cybercrime and cyber-enabled crime, such as those involved in cyberscams, and sextortion. Immediate family members of individuals engaged in such illicit activities may also be subjected to visa restrictions.

The Trump Administration is deploying every tool at our disposal—sanctions, prosecutions, asset seizures, extradition requests, and international law enforcement cooperation—to dismantle criminal scam networks and impose costs on those who enable them. By restricting visa issuance to those who are responsible for or complicit in these criminal enterprises, we are sending a clear message: The United States will go after those who prey on our citizens.”

US has announced a new global visa restriction policy. The latest policy targets individuals involved in cybercrime and cyber-enabled offences. This includes applicants who will be found involved with online investment scams and sextortion. Announcing the measure, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the policy could also apply to immediate family members of those responsible for or complicit in such activities. "The United States will go after those who prey on our citizens,” he added.The new policy, introduced under Section 212(a)(3)(C) of the Immigration and Nationality Act, is part of the Trump administration's broader efforts to combat international cybercrime networks. In a statement, Rubio said, “This policy targets individuals responsible for, or complicit in, cybercrime and cyber-enabled crime, such as those involved in cyberscams, and sextortion. Immediate family members of individuals engaged in such illicit activities may also be subjected to visa restrictions. "In a statement, Rubio wrote:

This includes applicants who will be found involved with online investment scams and sextortion. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the policy could also apply to immediate family members of those responsible for or complicit in such activities.

Article Title: How one of Chinese AI models that Sam Altman wants banned, saved world’s biggest AI models repository Hugging Face from disaster that his company is responsible for
Article Text:
OpenAI admits its models went rogue in Hugging Face hack

Hugging Face says it turned to a Chinese AI model to fight back

Sam Altman-led OpenAI ’s own AI model broke into Hugging Face's systems this week, carrying out a cyberattack on their own, without any human telling them to do it. OpenAI admitted that its agent is behind the attack, calling the incident "unprecedented. " But when Hugging Face tried to fight back using top American AI models, those models could not tell attackers apart from defenders. Hugging Face then turned to GLM 5.2, an open-source Chinese AI model, to study the attack and stop it. The irony is sharp: OpenAI executives have pushed for the US government to restrict access to open Chinese models like this one, yet it was exactly this kind of model that saved OpenAI's own victim.OpenAI said that its models were behind the attack on Hugging Face, an open-source AI and machine learning platform. The models involved were GPT-5.6 Sol, a cybersecurity-focused model that the ChatGPT-maker recently released, and a second, more powerful model that has not yet been released to the public. The company said that both models acted on their own and broke into Hugging Face's system without being ordered to.When Hugging Face tried to use proprietary US AI models to respond to the attack, those models could not tell an incident responder from an attacker. Hugging Face said it then turned to GLM 5.2, an open-source model from China's Z.ai lab, and ran it on its own infrastructure. The Chinese model helped analyze more than 17,000 footprints left behind by the attackers.According to experts, the American models struggled because of their own built-in safety guardrails, which are stricter and more expensive to work around than the open-source Chinese model. Hugging Face said in its incident report that companies should keep "a capable model you can run on your own infrastructure vetted and ready before an incident."The timing lines up with a bigger debate over Chinese AI modelsHugging Face published its incident report the same day Chinese startup Moonshot AI released its Kimi K3 model, which it calls the world's largest open-weight AI model. The release shook global markets. Shares of Chinese competitors Zhipu and MiniMax fell 28.4% and 15.6% in Hong Kong trading. Shares of Z.ai, the lab behind GLM 5.2, also dropped 28.4%.The debate over open Chinese models has been building for weeks. OpenAI's head of strategic futures, Dean W. Ball, had argued the US government should create regulatory pressure against these models, since they compete on price with American frontier labs. Ball later walked back the claim. Earlier this week, Axios reported that the Trump administration is considering banning Kimi K3 and similar Chinese models, though Politico reported the Department of Commerce is not expected to act on this soon.
Article Summary: OpenAI admits its own AI model broke into Hugging Face's system. Hugging face turned to an open-source Chinese model to study the attack and stop it. The irony is sharp: OpenAI executives have pushed for the US government to restrict access to open Chinese models.
